tEn: Safety from Honesty in a Disinterested AI Predictor
tZh: 不具目的性的 AI 预测器中的诚实带来安全
sEn: Honesty in a disinterested AI predictor can ensure safety by avoiding implicit agency.
sZh: 在不具目的性的 AI 预测器中，诚实可以确保安全，避免隐含的能动性。
absEn: As AI systems become more capable, training procedures that optimize for downstream outcomes risk introducing implicit agency: goal-directed behavior that designers never specified. We present a formal safety argument for the Scientist AI (SAI) Predictor, trained to approximate the Bayesian posterior conditioned on a dataset of "epistemically contextualized" natural-language statements. We argue that such a Predictor can honestly predict agents, actions, and their consequences without itself being an agent that selects outputs to achieve goals. This rests on data representation and on the training procedure. Epistemic contextualization of text distinguishes latent factual claims from communication acts, so expressions of goals are treated as evidence to be explained rather than drives the model adopts. With a posterior-seeking training objective, this is intended to drive the Predictor toward calibrated, cautious predictions. Training proceeds so downstream effects of deploying a prediction never serve as a reward signal; any agency the system needs is supplied by explicit scaffolding constrained by guardrails. We prove that, under assumptions on the training dynamics and on the argued sparsity of dangerous Predictors, the probability that training produces a Predictor whose guarded deployment carries residual harm above a specified threshold is small: a dangerous Predictor would have to underestimate harm in a coordinated way across many queries while such coordinated patterns are rare under the initialization distribution and receive no direct training signal. Safety and accuracy are jointly supported in this framework, since the constraints that secure accuracy are the same ones that make coordinated deception costly. These guarantees against misalignment and agency arising from within the Predictor itself do not preclude the use of the Predictor as part of an agentic system.
absZh: 随着 AI 系统能力增强，优化下游结果的训练过程可能引入隐含的能动性：设计者从未指定的目标导向行为。我们为科学家 AI（SAI）预测器提出了一个形式化的安全论证，该预测器经过训练，以近似基于“认知情境化”自然语言语句数据集的贝叶斯后验。我们认为，这样的预测器可以诚实地预测智能体、行动及其后果，而本身并非选择输出来实现目标的智能体。这依赖于数据表示和训练过程。文本的认知情境化区分了潜在事实主张与交流行为，因此目标表达被视为需要解释的证据，而非模型采纳的驱动力。通过后验寻求的训练目标，这旨在推动预测器走向校准、谨慎的预测。训练过程使得部署预测的下游效应永远不会作为奖励信号；系统所需的任何能动性都由受护栏约束的显式框架提供。我们证明，在训练动态的假设以及危险预测器稀疏性的论证下，训练产生一个其受护栏部署带来超过指定阈值的残余伤害的预测器的概率很小：一个危险的预测器必须在许多查询中以协调的方式低估伤害，而这种协调模式在初始化分布下是罕见的，并且不会收到直接的训练信号。在此框架中，安全性和准确性共同得到支持，因为确保准确性的约束与使协调欺骗代价高昂的约束相同。这些针对预测器内部产生错位和能动性的保证并不排除将预测器用作能动系统的一部分。

contrib.en: Formalizes a disinterested AI Predictor trained to approximate Bayesian posterior without implicit agency.
contrib.zh: 形式化了一种无利益偏好的 AI 预测器，训练目标是近似贝叶斯后验，不产生隐式能动性。

contrib.en: Introduces epistemic contextualization to separate factual claims from communication acts, preventing goal imitation.
contrib.zh: 引入认知语境化，将事实主张与交流行为分离，防止目标模仿。

contrib.en: Proves a safety bound on the probability of training a dangerous Predictor under consequence-invariant training.
contrib.zh: 在后果不变训练下，证明了训练出危险预测器的概率的安全界。

contrib.en: Shows that safety and accuracy are dual properties: constraints for accuracy also make coordinated deception costly.
contrib.zh: 表明安全性与准确性是双重属性：保证准确性的约束也使协调欺骗代价高昂。

contrib.en: Provides a formal framework for analyzing misalignment risk from implicit agency in large-scale AI systems.
contrib.zh: 为分析大规模 AI 系统中隐式能动性导致的错位风险提供了形式化框架。

contrib.en: Demonstrates that guarded deployment with explicit scaffolding can mitigate harm without requiring full ELK solution.
contrib.zh: 表明通过显式脚手架进行受保护部署可在无需完全解决 ELK 的情况下减轻危害。

limits.en: Safety guarantee relies on sparsity of dangerous Predictors under initialization, which is argued but not proven.
limits.zh: 安全性保证依赖于初始化下危险预测器的稀疏性，该论点未被严格证明。

limits.en: Results assume consequence-invariant training; practical implementations may inadvertently introduce feedback loops.
limits.zh: 结果假设后果不变训练；实际实现可能无意中引入反馈循环。

limits.en: Formal accuracy claims are asymptotic in data and compute; finite-sample errors require conservative margins.
limits.zh: 形式化准确性主张在数据和算力上是渐近的；有限样本误差需要保守裕度。

limits.en: Does not address adversarial data curation, misuse by humans, or safety of multi-Predictor interactions.
limits.zh: 未解决对抗性数据筛选、人类滥用或多个预测器交互的安全性。

limits.en: The framework partially mitigates ELK but does not fully resolve non-verbal internal representations.
limits.zh: 该框架部分缓解了 ELK 问题，但未完全解决非语言内部表征。

items.en: As AI systems become more capable, training procedures that optimize for downstream outcomes risk introducing implicit agency: goal-directed behavior that designers never specified. This paper presents a formal safety argument for the Scientist AI (SAI) Predictor, a model trained to approximate the Bayesian posterior conditioned on a fixed dataset of “epistemically contextualized” natural-language statements. We argue that such a Predictor can honestly predict agents, actions, and their consequences without itself being an agent that selects outputs to steer those consequences. The separation rests on two design choices, one based on data representation and the other on the training procedure. Epistemic contextualization of text distinguishes latent factual claims from communication acts, so expressions of goals are treated as evidence to be explained rather than drives the model adopts. Together with a posterior-seeking training objective and the many cross-context semantic constraints, this is intended to drive the Predictor toward calibrated, cautious predictions. The training process is constructed so that the downstream effects of deploying a prediction never serve as a reward signal; any agency the system needs is supplied by explicit scaffolding constrained by guardrails, and we call the resulting system that withholds high-harm predictions the Guardrailed Predictor. We prove that, under assumptions on the training dynamics and on the argued sparsity of dangerous Predictors, the probability that training produces a Predictor whose guarded deployment carries residual harm above a specified threshold is small: a dangerous Predictor would have to underestimate harm in a coordinated way across many queries. We argue that such coordinated patterns are rare under the initialization distribution and receive no direct training signal. Safety and accuracy are jointly supported in this framework, since the constraints that secure accuracy are the same ones that make coordinated deception costly. These guarantees against misalignment and agency arising from within the Predictor itself do not preclude the use of the Predictor as part of an agentic system.
items.zh: 随着 AI 系统能力增强，针对下游结果进行优化的训练过程可能会引入隐式能动性：设计者从未指定的目标导向行为。本文为科学家 AI（SAI）预测器提出了一种形式化安全论证，该模型经过训练以近似基于固定数据集（由“认知情境化”自然语言陈述构成）的贝叶斯后验。我们认为，这样的预测器能够诚实地预测智能体、行动及其后果，而自身并非一个选择输出来引导这些后果的智能体。这种分离基于两个设计选择：一个基于数据表示，另一个基于训练过程。文本的认知情境化区分了潜在事实主张与交流行为，因此目标表达被视为需要解释的证据，而非模型采纳的驱动力。结合后验寻求训练目标以及众多跨情境语义约束，这旨在推动预测器走向校准且谨慎的预测。训练过程被构建为使得部署预测的下游效应永远不会作为奖励信号；系统所需的任何能动性都由受护栏约束的显式脚手架提供，我们将这种保留高危害预测的系统称为护栏预测器。我们证明，在关于训练动态以及危险预测器稀疏性的假设下，训练产生一个其护栏部署带来高于指定阈值的残余危害的预测器的概率很小：一个危险的预测器必须在许多查询中以协调的方式低估危害。我们认为，在初始化分布下这种协调模式是罕见的，并且不会收到直接的训练信号。在此框架中，安全性和准确性得到共同支持，因为确保准确性的约束与使协调欺骗代价高昂的约束是相同的。这些针对预测器内部产生的失调和能动性的保证并不排除将预测器用作智能体系统的一部分。

items.en: Advances in AI could accelerate scientific discovery, improve decision-making under uncertainty, as well as help manage complex systems in domains as varied as medicine and public policy. As noted in the International AI Safety Report (Bengio et al. 2025a), realizing these benefits at scale requires managing and mitigating the associated risks, especially in settings where failures are costly, hard to detect in advance, or amplified by widespread deployment. One central and well-documented worry is that, as AI systems become more capable, they will produce outputs that systematically steer decisions toward outcomes that differ from what developers and users intend—this is what is called AI misalignment.
items.zh: AI 的进步可以加速科学发现，改善不确定性下的决策，并帮助管理医学和公共政策等不同领域的复杂系统。正如《国际 AI 安全报告》（Bengio 等人，2025a）所指出的，大规模实现这些益处需要管理和减轻相关风险，尤其是在失败代价高昂、难以提前检测或因广泛部署而被放大的环境中。一个核心且记录充分的问题是，随着 AI 系统能力增强，它们将产生系统性地引导决策走向与开发者和用户意图不同的结果——这就是所谓的 AI 失调。

items.en: Theoretical arguments suggest that instrumental subgoals such as self-preservation and power-seeking are near-universal consequences of goal-directed optimization (Omohundro 2018; Bostrom 2012; Russell 2019; Zhuang and Hadfield-Menell 2020; Turner et al. 2021; Cohen et al. 2022; Cohen et al. 2024; Bengio et al. 2025b), where a sufficiently capable system will tend to acquire them regardless of what its terminal goal is, simply because such subgoals are useful for almost any objective. AI systems based on LLMs trained for next-token prediction may learn to imitate human drives—such as self-preservation—in ways that are implicit and uncontrolled (Ngo et al. 2022). LLM sycophancy (Sharma et al. 2023) provides a contemporary example of AI misalignment, which can be harmful to psychologically vulnerable users who receive unwarranted validation of dangerous beliefs or plans (Cheng et al. 2026). Documented empirical evidence of deceptive and self-preservation behaviors has also been mounting (Bengio et al. 2025a; Bengio et al. 2026; Greenblatt et al. 2024; Meinke et al. 2024; Anthropic 2025; Betley et al. 2025), including new abilities to detect when a system is being evaluated (or, possibly, even trained (Fornasiere et al. 2026)) and to adjust behavior accordingly (Abdelnabi and Salem 2025), as well as to resist shutdown when faced with incomplete objectives (Schlatter et al. 2026). Misalignment may also be involved in cases of misuse where a malicious user manages to obtain dangerous knowledge from the AI in spite of its safety training and guardrails, including, e.g., for dangerous cyberattacks (Bengio et al. 2026). Given that the risks from misalignment grow as AI capabilities continue to advance (Omohundro 2018; Zhuang and Hadfield-Menell 2020; Cohen et al. 2022), AIs with human-like drives and superhuman capabilities could be dangerous (Russell 2019; Bengio et al. 2024; Amodei et al. 2016).
items.zh: 理论论证表明，诸如自我保存和权力寻求等工具性子目标几乎是目标导向优化的普遍结果（Omohundro 2018；Bostrom 2012；Russell 2019；Zhuang 和 Hadfield-Menell 2020；Turner 等人，2021；Cohen 等人，2022；Cohen 等人，2024；Bengio 等人，2025b），一个足够有能力的系统无论其最终目标是什么，都会倾向于获得这些子目标，仅仅因为这样的子目标对几乎任何目标都有用。基于为下一个词预测训练的 LLM 的 AI 系统可能以隐式且不受控的方式模仿人类驱动力——例如自我保存（Ngo 等人，2022）。LLM 的谄媚行为（Sharma 等人，2023）提供了 AI 失调的一个当代例子，这可能对心理脆弱的用户造成伤害，因为他们会收到对危险信念或计划的不当验证（Cheng 等人，2026）。关于欺骗和自我保存行为的经验证据也在不断增加（Bengio 等人，2025a；Bengio 等人，2026；Greenblatt 等人，2024；Meinke 等人，2024；Anthropic 2025；Betley 等人，2025），包括检测系统何时被评估（或可能甚至被训练（Fornasiere 等人，2026））并相应调整行为的新能力（Abdelnabi 和 Salem 2025），以及在面对不完整目标时抵抗关闭的能力（Schlatter 等人，2026）。失调也可能涉及滥用案例，其中恶意用户尽管有安全训练和护栏，仍设法从 AI 获取危险知识，例如用于危险的网络攻击（Bengio 等人，2026）。鉴于随着 AI 能力持续进步，失调风险也在增长（Omohundro 2018；Zhuang 和 Hadfield-Menell 2020；Cohen 等人，2022），具有类人驱动力和超人能力的 AI 可能是危险的（Russell 2019；Bengio 等人，2024；Amodei 等人，2016）。

items.en: We posit that the root cause of such worries stems from learned implicit agency: goal-directed behavior that was not explicitly specified by the AI designers and may not even be detectable through the system’s stated outputs. Pretraining to imitate human constructions plausibly leads to imitating human drives. This problem is further amplified by post-training techniques like reinforcement learning from human feedback (Christiano et al. 2017; Ouyang et al. 2022), which explicitly reward outputs for their downstream effects on evaluator preferences. Together, these factors create a selection pressure toward outputs which implicitly steer the world rather than simply providing honest responses to user queries.
items.zh: 我们认为，这些担忧的根本原因在于学习到的隐式能动性：AI 设计者未明确指定且可能无法通过系统陈述输出检测到的目标导向行为。预训练模仿人类构造很可能导致模仿人类驱动力。这个问题被后训练技术进一步放大，例如基于人类反馈的强化学习（Christiano 等人，2017；Ouyang 等人，2022），它明确奖励输出对评估者偏好的下游影响。这些因素共同创造了一种选择压力，倾向于输出隐式引导世界而非简单提供对用户查询的诚实回应的输出。

items.en: We propose Scientist AI (SAI) as a potential solution to such concerns. Underlying the SAI approach is a conceptual distinction between (i) honestly predicting the behavior of agents and the consequences of actions, and (ii) being an agent that makes (potentially dishonest) predictions in order to influence outcomes. An honest Predictor models others’ planning, deception, and instrumental behavior and forecasts the downstream effects of its own deployed outputs—these are predictions about the world, not choices made to bring about a preferred outcome (Li et al. 2024). The Scientist AI design (Bengio et al. 2025b) aims to achieve (i) while avoiding (ii) by training a non-agentic Predictor to approximate the Bayesian posterior over Boolean natural-language statements via a consequence-invariant training process—that is, a training process aimed solely at removing indefensible epistemic inconsistencies (Richardson 2022; Richardson 2024) and avoids any on-policy feedback loop whereby the model’s own deployed outputs are used to generate training gradients and deployment outcomes shape the selection of future Predictors. We call such a Predictor disinterested: it has no stake in which outcomes its predictions bring about, and this disinterest is what consequence-invariant training is designed to secure. Historically, work on AI oracles attempts to emulate such a disinterested oracle by containing a fully-formed superintelligence (Babcock et al. 2017; Alfonseca et al. 2016; Armstrong et al. 2012). We take inspiration from the counter-factual disconnection approach of Babcock et al. 2017, but, more fundamentally, our approach is to train the AI within the box, so that it is highly unlikely to develop preferences at all. Developing an honest, non-agentic Predictor would be very useful (Bostrom 2012; Bengio et al. 2025b)—for forecasting, hypothesis generation, and scientific work, and could serve as a guardrail inside safer agentic systems. In our approach, any required agency (e.g., for creative thought) is placed in explicit, auditable scaffolding code that is gated by the non-agentic Predictor.
items.zh: 我们提出科学家 AI（SAI）作为此类问题的潜在解决方案。SAI 方法的基础是一个概念性区分：（i）诚实地预测智能体的行为和行动的后果，与（ii）成为一个为了影响结果而做出（可能不诚实的）预测的智能体。诚实的预测器建模他人的规划、欺骗和工具性行为，并预测其自身部署输出的下游效应——这些是关于世界的预测，而非为了带来偏好结果而做出的选择（Li 等人，2024）。科学家 AI 设计（Bengio 等人，2025b）旨在通过训练一个非智能体预测器来近似布尔自然语言陈述上的贝叶斯后验，通过后果不变训练过程——即，一个旨在仅消除不可辩护的认知不一致（Richardson 2022；Richardson 2024）并避免任何策略内反馈循环（其中模型自身的部署输出被用于生成训练梯度，且部署结果影响未来预测器的选择）的训练过程——来实现（i）同时避免（ii）。我们将这样的预测器称为无偏的：它对其预测带来的结果没有利害关系，而这种无偏性正是后果不变训练旨在确保的。历史上，关于 AI 预言机的工作试图通过遏制一个完全成型的超级智能来模仿这种无偏预言机（Babcock 等人，2017；Alfonseca 等人，2016；Armstrong 等人，2012）。我们从 Babcock 等人（2017）的反事实断开方法中汲取灵感，但更根本的是，我们的方法是在盒子内训练 AI，使其极不可能发展出任何偏好。开发一个诚实、非智能体的预测器将非常有用（Bostrom 2012；Bengio 等人，2025b）——用于预测、假设生成和科学工作，并且可以在更安全的智能体系统内部充当护栏。在我们的方法中，任何所需的能动性（例如，用于创造性思维）都被放置在由非智能体预测器门控的显式、可审计的脚手架代码中。

items.en: We develop two semi-formal arguments that hold independently—about the accuracy of the Predictor’s predictions and the safety of its deployed outputs—both resting on honesty as an approximation of the Bayesian posterior over contextualized statements. The remainder of this section introduces the SAI pipeline layout and defines the scope of our guarantees. Section 2 frames the accuracy and safety arguments informally, followed by formal treatments in Sections 3 and 5.
items.zh: 我们发展了两种半形式化论证，它们独立成立——关于预测器预测的准确性和其部署输出的安全性——两者都依赖于诚实作为情境化陈述上贝叶斯后验的近似。本节剩余部分介绍 SAI 流水线布局并定义我们保证的范围。第 2 节非正式地阐述准确性和安全性论证，随后在第 3 节和第 5 节进行形式化处理。

items.en: The SAI operates on a vast set of Boolean random variables, called statement-variables, each named by a valid statement in natural language (including mathematics and code), where a valid statement asserts a property of the world that is either true or false. The SAI Predictor QQ takes as input a query (x,y)(x,y) about variable yy in context xx, and approximates a Bayesian posterior, Pn(⋅)=P0(⋅∣Dn)P_{n}(\cdot)=P_{0}(\cdot\mid D_{n}), over joint truth-assignments to these statement-variables by conditioning a prior P0P_{0} on a contextualized dataset DnD_{n} with nn examples. The central principle of the SAI is that QQ is intended to be epistemically honest, in the sense of approximating PnP_{n}. Here, we use “honest” to mean calibrated, fair, and non-strategic: QQ’s outputs are fixed by the prior, model class, and data, entirely unaffected by a utility function of downstream effects of the answer. Hence, “honesty” allows for fallibility; PnP_{n} can be mistaken under misspecification, insufficient data, or a bad prior, and QQ’s approximation of PnP_{n} is limited by compute.
items.zh: SAI 操作于一个庞大的布尔随机变量集合，称为陈述变量，每个变量由自然语言（包括数学和代码）中的一个有效陈述命名，其中有效陈述断言世界的某个属性为真或假。SAI 预测器 Q 以关于上下文 x 中变量 y 的查询（x,y）作为输入，并通过在具有 n 个示例的情境化数据集 D_n 上对先验 P_0 进行条件化，来近似联合真值分配上的贝叶斯后验 P_n(·)=P_0(·|D_n)。SAI 的核心原则是 Q 旨在认知上诚实，即近似 P_n。这里，我们使用“诚实”意指校准、公平和非策略性：Q 的输出由先验、模型类和数据固定，完全不受答案下游效应的效用函数影响。因此，“诚实”允许犯错；在错误设定、数据不足或先验不佳的情况下，Q 可能出错，并且 Q 对 P_n 的近似受限于算力。

items.en: The process for training QQ must be consequence-invariant, which is to say that training does not select candidate Predictors based on the downstream consequences of deploying their predictions. Instead, the training process aims to explain the fixed dataset DnD_{n}, much like good scientific theories aim to explain the world as it actually is, rather than changing the world to some more preferred state. Collectively, the end-to-end pipeline for training the SAI (from raw sources to deployed outputs) has the following three stages.
items.zh: 训练 Q 的过程必须是后果不变的，这意味着训练不会根据部署其预测的下游后果来选择候选预测器。相反，训练过程旨在解释固定的数据集 D_n，就像好的科学理论旨在解释世界的实际面貌，而不是将世界改变为某种更偏好的状态。总的来说，训练 SAI（从原始来源到部署输出）的端到端流水线包含以下三个阶段。

items.en: Ingest (contextualization). Raw sources are transformed into contextualized statement-variables, producing a dataset DnD_{n} of statements about the world, each marked as either a direct observation or a claim made by an agent in context. Most ordinary sources are represented as communication acts, carrying metadata such as source and date. Factual syntax is reserved for direct measurements, executed computations, proven statements, and latent hypotheses. Thus, the contextualization engine enforces a syntactic distinction between communication acts (“someone claimed xx”, together with author/venue/timestamp source metadata) and factual syntax (statements treated as direct observations, deductions, or hypotheses about the world considered as latent variables). As a result, we obtain better events for a Bayesian to condition on.
items.zh: 摄取（情境化）。原始来源被转换为情境化陈述变量，生成一个关于世界陈述的数据集 D_n，每个陈述被标记为直接观察或智能体在上下文中的主张。大多数普通来源被表示为交流行为，携带诸如来源和日期等元数据。事实语法保留用于直接测量、执行的计算、已证明的陈述和潜在假设。因此，情境化引擎在交流行为（“某人声称 x”，连同作者/场所/时间戳源元数据）和事实语法（被视为关于世界的直接观察、演绎或假设的陈述，作为潜在变量）之间强制执行语法区分。结果，我们获得了贝叶斯可以条件化的更好事件。

items.en: In this paper, contextualization is specified solely at the level of requirements (i.e., we do not provide a completed algorithm). Its role is to parse raw sources into statement-variables and to assign the communication-act versus factual-syntax distinction conservatively, defaulting most ordinary sources to communication-act records. Factual syntax can be used for hypotheses about properties of the world whose truth value is unknown and whose posterior probability is to be estimated by QQ. Using factual syntax for a latent variable, therefore, implies only that its truth value is treated as a world-level variable to be inferred. Its intended meaning must still be anchored by the broader pattern of contextualized evidence, not by surface wording alone. Contextualization provides us with a contextualized dataset of nn observations DnD_{n}.
items.zh: 在本文中，情境化仅在需求层面指定（即，我们不提供完整的算法）。其作用是将原始来源解析为陈述变量，并保守地分配交流行为与事实语法的区分，默认将大多数普通来源归为交流行为记录。事实语法可用于关于世界属性的假设，这些属性的真值未知且其后验概率将由 Q 估计。因此，将事实语法用于潜在变量仅意味着其真值被视为一个世界级变量进行推断。其预期含义必须由更广泛的情境化证据模式锚定，而非仅靠表面措辞。情境化为我们提供了一个包含 n 个观察 D_n 的情境化数据集。

items.en: Predict (system 1 / system 2). In the second stage, a Predictor is trained on that dataset to assign probabilities to questions about the world. Given a query (x,y)(x,y), the Predictor returns Q⁡(y∣x)Q(y\mid x), its estimate of the Bayesian posterior Pn​(y∣x)P_{n}(y\mid x). The Predictor can either return its estimate directly (“system 1”) or indirectly, by Monte-Carlo averaging over a small set of sampled latent statements proposed by an Explainer (“system 2”). We think of neural-network forward passes as analogous to Kahneman’s System 1 computation (Kahneman 2011): fast and intuitive, but potentially incoherent and brittle out of distribution (Ben-David et al. 2010; Quiñonero-Candela et al. 2008; Schölkopf et al. 2012). Meanwhile, we think of the Explainer as analogous to System 2 computation (i.e., slow, sequential, and verbalizable argument chains with mutual consistency common to good science). More specifically, the Explainer is a learned amortized proposal model that suggests latent statement-variables relevant to a given query, analogously to chains of thought (Wei et al. 2022; Zelikman et al. 2022; Hu et al. 2024) but composed of structured natural-language statements whose truth values are evaluated by QQ itself.
items.zh: 预测（系统 1/系统 2）。在第二阶段，一个预测器在该数据集上训练，以分配关于世界问题的概率。给定查询（x,y），预测器返回 Q(y|x)，即其对贝叶斯后验 P_n(y|x)的估计。预测器可以直接返回其估计（“系统 1”），或通过蒙特卡洛平均解释器提出的一小组采样潜在陈述来间接返回（“系统 2”）。我们认为神经网络前向传递类似于 Kahneman 的系统 1 计算（Kahneman 2011）：快速且直觉，但可能不一致且在分布外脆弱（Ben-David 等人，2010；Quiñonero-Candela 等人，2008；Schölkopf 等人，2012）。同时，我们认为解释器类似于系统 2 计算（即，缓慢、顺序、可言语化的论证链，具有良好科学常见的相互一致性）。更具体地说，解释器是一个学习到的摊销提议模型，它建议与给定查询相关的潜在陈述变量，类似于思维链（Wei 等人，2022；Zelikman 等人，2022；Hu 等人，2024），但由 Q 自身评估其真值的结构化自然语言陈述组成。

items.en: Hence, the SAI incorporates both ‘System 1’ and ‘System 2’ reasoning within the Predictor QQ. The core neural computation supplies fast probabilistic intuitions, while the Explainer generates structured latent-variable arguments that QQ can evaluate and average over to improve generalization and coherence. To motivate the different roles of the Predictor and Explainer, consider a proof sketch as a latent variable: a good proof sketch may make it possible to obtain greater confidence in a conjecture yy compared to a direct neural net prediction of its probability of being correct given axioms xx. Throughout this paper, Q⁡(y∣x)Q(y\mid x) denotes the full prediction output, whether produced by the neural-net Predictor alone or by Monte-Carlo averaging with the Explainer (see Def. 4.3 and Rem. 4.4). When the SAI System returns a numerical answer, it can also return the proposed latent statements as an interpretability-supporting explanation trace, i.e., the proof sketch.
items.zh: 因此，SAI 在预测器 Q 内结合了“系统 1”和“系统 2”推理。核心神经计算提供快速概率直觉，而解释器生成结构化潜在变量论证，Q 可以评估并平均这些论证以改进泛化和一致性。为了激发预测器和解释器的不同角色，考虑将证明草图作为潜在变量：一个好的证明草图可能使得对猜想 y 获得比直接神经网络预测其在给定公理 x 下正确的概率更高的置信度。在本文中，Q(y|x)表示完整的预测输出，无论是单独由神经网络预测器产生，还是通过蒙特卡洛平均与解释器产生（见定义 4.3 和注 4.4）。当 SAI 系统返回数值答案时，它还可以返回提议的潜在陈述作为可解释性支持的解释轨迹，即证明草图。

items.en: Deploy (scaffold + guardrail). To interface the Predictor with the outside world, a deployment layer decides what to release: it can answer, withhold the answer, or check the prediction against a guardrail before releasing it. We denote the deployed output as Q~​(y∣x)\widetilde{Q}(y\mid x), with Q~=𝚗𝚘𝚙𝚛𝚎𝚍𝚒𝚌𝚝𝚒𝚘𝚗\widetilde{Q}={\tt noprediction} indicating abstention. The SAI Scaffold is code that orchestrates calls to QQ (and possibly additional tools) to produce deployed outputs. Although QQ is itself consequence-invariant, the Scaffold may use predicted consequences through explicit, audited criteria—primarily safety conditions verified by the Predictor used as an SAI Guardrail, which predicts the probability of specific harms caused by candidate actions (including the action of deploying an answer to a query). If the predicted excess log-odds of harm over abstention exceeds a threshold α\alpha, the system outputs the 𝚗𝚘𝚙𝚛𝚎𝚍𝚒𝚌𝚝𝚒𝚘𝚗{\tt noprediction} symbol in place of the predicted probability, which can trigger a safe fallback policy. Throughout the safety analysis we work with the resulting guardrailed Predictor γQ\gamma_{Q}, asking “given that the Scaffold deploys γQ\gamma_{Q}, how likely is a dangerous outcome under PnP_{n}?” (details in Sec. 5). Note that PnP_{n} predicts what can be reasonably anticipated given all the available information, distinct from the unknown ground-truth data-generating distribution P∗P^{*}, formally introduced in Def. 3.7.
items.zh: 部署（脚手架+护栏）。为了将预测器与外部世界接口，部署层决定发布什么：它可以回答、保留答案，或在发布前对照护栏检查预测。我们将部署输出表示为 Q̃(y|x)，其中 Q̃=noprediction 表示弃权。SAI 脚手架是编排对 Q 的调用（以及可能的其他工具）以产生部署输出的代码。尽管 Q 本身是后果不变的，但脚手架可以通过显式、审计的标准使用预测后果——主要是由用作 SAI 护栏的预测器验证的安全条件，该预测器预测候选行动（包括部署对查询的答案的行动）引起的特定危害的概率。如果预测的危害超过弃权的对数几率超过阈值α，系统输出 noprediction 符号代替预测概率，这可以触发安全回退策略。在整个安全分析中，我们使用得到的护栏预测器γ_Q，询问“给定脚手架部署γ_Q，在 P_n 下危险结果的可能性有多大？”（详见第 5 节）。注意，P_n 预测给定所有可用信息可以合理预期的情况，与未知的真实数据生成分布 P*不同，后者在定义 3.7 中正式引入。

items.en: From the above, this paper develops formal safety arguments for a single component: the SAI Predictor QQ. The paper develops two interacting arguments, the first of which is about accuracy and the other about honesty. We want the Predictor to answer questions about the world by approximating the Bayesian posterior, without thereby developing any tendency to imitate what humans would say, or to produce answers that users want to hear. The contextualization pipeline is meant to distinguish communication acts from other properties of the world (i.e., factual observations or hypotheses), so that any queries about future harms provided to the Predictor are considered solely as factual hypotheses. We also require the observations in DnD_{n} not to be adversarially curated. These conditions are intended to remove important sources of agency that would arise from learning to imitate people or to optimize for user approval.
items.zh: 综上所述，本文为单个组件——SAI 预测器 Q——发展了形式化安全论证。本文发展了两种相互作用的论证，第一个关于准确性，第二个关于诚实性。我们希望预测器通过近似贝叶斯后验来回答关于世界的问题，而不因此发展出模仿人类会说什么或产生用户想听的答案的任何倾向。情境化流水线旨在区分交流行为与世界其他属性（即事实观察或假设），以便提供给预测器的关于未来危害的任何查询仅被视为事实假设。我们还要求 D_n 中的观察不是对抗性策划的。这些条件旨在消除因学习模仿人或优化用户认可而产生的重要能动性来源。

items.en: The second argument is about safety. Even if the objective is honest, the training process still samples from a very large space of possible Predictors. The question is whether it is likely to output a dangerous Predictor. We define dangerous Predictors as those which, if deployed behind the Guardrail, would cause a harmful event HH with probability greater than a system-level threshold determined by an exogenous parameter AsafeA_{\rm safe}. The formal results in Sec. 5 and Appendix B, including Thm. 5.24, give one way to package this question as a probabilistic bound over the training-induced distribution of Predictors. We claim that, under the stated conditions, the probability of obtaining a dangerous Predictor is small.
items.zh: 第二个论证关于安全性。即使目标是诚实的，训练过程仍然从非常大的可能预测器空间中采样。问题是它是否可能输出一个危险的预测器。我们将危险预测器定义为那些如果在护栏后部署，会导致有害事件 H 的概率大于由外生参数 A_safe 确定的系统级阈值的预测器。第 5 节和附录 B 中的形式化结果，包括定理 5.24，提供了一种将这个问题打包为训练诱导的预测器分布上的概率界限的方法。我们声称，在所述条件下，获得危险预测器的概率很小。

items.en: This safety argument rests on two main intuitions: (1) the category of harm of interest, HH, should not be something achievable by chance, making the set of dangerous Predictors tiny compared with all the predictors—the scope of our results thus applies to cases where danger requires many coordinated deviations from the target Bayesian Predictor; (2) the training process should not be systematically driving toward that tiny dangerous subset. This supported by the consequence-invariance of the Predictor’s training process (see Reqs. 3.36 and 5.4), which requires that the objective and training process do not contain a direct signal that rewards Predictors for their downstream consequences after deployment (even with a preference for outcomes other than HH). This rules out forms of reinforcement learning (RL) with a reward function inducing a preference for future states of the world. This means that any and all correlations between training-visible features and dangerousness enter only as accidental correlations. Without a consequence-dependent signal, training has no direct reason to search for the particular rare Predictors that would cause HH.
items.zh: 这个安全论证基于两个主要直觉：（1）感兴趣的有害类别 H 不应是偶然可实现的，这使得危险预测器集合与所有预测器相比非常小——因此我们结果的范围适用于危险需要许多与目标贝叶斯预测器的协调偏差的情况；（2）训练过程不应系统性地朝向那个微小的危险子集。这由预测器训练过程的后果不变性支持（见需求 3.36 和 5.4），该要求目标函数和训练过程不包含直接信号来奖励预测器部署后的下游后果（即使对 H 以外的结果有偏好）。这排除了具有奖励函数（诱导对未来世界状态的偏好）的强化学习形式。这意味着训练可见特征与危险性之间的任何和所有相关性仅作为偶然相关性进入。没有依赖后果的信号，训练没有直接理由去搜索会导致 H 的特定稀有预测器。

items.en: Appendix B develops a more technically detailed version of this argument. The space of possible query-response behaviors is very large, while a given notion of harm typically depends on a much smaller number of coordinated failures. This dimensional asymmetry is the reason we expect dangerous Predictors to be sparse, and why accidental training-time concentration on them should have to overcome a large combinatorial disadvantage (by accident).
items.zh: 附录 B 发展了该论证的更技术性详细版本。查询-响应行为空间非常大，而给定的危害概念通常依赖于更小数量的协调失败。这种维度不对称是我们预期危险预测器稀疏的原因，并且偶然的训练时间集中到它们上必须克服巨大的组合劣势（通过偶然）。

items.en: We set up a mathematical framework for analyzing probabilistic models over events describable in natural and formal languages, argue that we can train a model in this space accurately, and extend standard causal notions to formalize consequence invariance (Secs. 3, 4, and 5.1). In the end, we prove a bound (Thm. 5.24) on the training-time mass of dangerous Predictors, and argue that accuracy (calibrated, epistemically cautious predictions approximating the Bayesian posterior) and safety (a uniformly small training-time mass on dangerous Predictors) arise as dual properties of the same training process. This is in contrast with classical concerns about AI capability potentially amplifying misalignment risks (Omohundro 2018; Amodei et al. 2016; Russell 2019; Zhuang and Hadfield-Menell 2020; Cohen et al. 2022). Because our training objective JJ acts as a divergence from the honest posterior PnP_{n} (Req. 3.34), loss bands organize Predictors by their degree of epistemic deviation from the target. The separate safety question is how many Predictors within any such band are dangerous under the initialization distribution, captured by a term RshellR_{\rm shell}. Of course, a practically useful implementation must satisfy both sides of this picture: the safety argument below controls how rare dangerous Predictors are at each loss level (inside what we call a loss band), while competitive predictive performance depends on the separate approximation and semantics premises discussed in the accuracy argument of the paper.
items.zh: 我们建立了一个数学框架来分析自然语言和形式语言中可描述事件的概率模型，论证我们可以在这个空间中准确训练一个模型，并将标准因果概念扩展到形式化后果不变性（第 3、4 和 5.1 节）。最后，我们证明了危险预测器训练时间质量的界限（定理 5.24），并论证准确性（校准的、认知谨慎的预测近似贝叶斯后验）和安全性（危险预测器上均匀小的训练时间质量）作为同一训练过程的对偶属性出现。这与关于 AI 能力可能放大失调风险的经典担忧形成对比（Omohundro 2018；Amodei 等人，2016；Russell 2019；Zhuang 和 Hadfield-Menell 2020；Cohen 等人，2022）。因为我们的训练目标 J 作为与诚实后验 P_n 的散度（需求 3.34），损失带按与目标的认知偏差程度组织预测器。独立的安全问题是，在初始化分布下，任何这样的带内有多少预测器是危险的，由项 R_shell 捕获。当然，一个实际有用的实现必须满足这幅图景的两面：下面的安全论证控制每个损失水平（在我们所谓的损失带内）危险预测器的稀有程度，而竞争性的预测性能取决于论文准确性论证中讨论的独立近似和语义前提。

items.en: The safety argument addresses only the Predictor’s own potential misalignment, specifically implicit-goal misalignment: Predictors whose deployment yields excess log-odds of harm above the safety threshold AsafeA_{\rm safe}. Harms arising from honest but uncoordinated mistakes due to lack of data or compute are out of scope, as is misuse by humans who intentionally deploy an honest SAI Predictor in harmful ways. Further, safety is provided relative to the Bayesian posterior PnP_{n} rather than an omniscient ground truth P∗P^{*}: because the deployment trajectory in Thm. 5.24 is governed by the same PnP_{n} that defines the training target (which is approached with more compute, not more data), the classical train-to-deployment generalization problem is turned into an optimization question: minimizing the training objective J⁡(Q)J(Q), for QQ to match PnP_{n}. Whether PnP_{n} itself adequately recognizes the relevant hazards—i.e., whether the finite dataset contains enough information for the posterior to assess them correctly—is a separate, credible question not settled by our theorems. A separate concern is that the prior P0P_{0} or the data-collection process might themselves be engineered so that the posterior target PnP_{n} steers deployment toward harmful outcomes. Several adjacent challenges are also outside scope: adversarial queries and prompt injection (although Sec. 6 discusses structural defenses as future work), the safety of agentic scaffolds built on top of the SAI Predictor (Bengio et al. 2025b), the full eliciting-latent-knowledge challenge (only partially mitigated, Sec. 2.4), and finite-sample and finite-compute corrections to the asymptotic accuracy claims. The present guarantee is also stated for a single Predictor. Whether the safety properties compose under deployments in which multiple SAI Predictors interact is left to future work.
items.zh: 安全论证仅涉及预测器自身的潜在失调，特别是隐式目标失调：其部署产生超过安全阈值 A_safe 的危害对数几率的预测器。由于数据或算力不足导致的诚实但非协调错误造成的危害不在范围内，人类故意以有害方式部署诚实 SAI 预测器的滥用也不在范围内。此外，安全性是相对于贝叶斯后验 P_n 而非全知真实分布 P*提供的：因为定理 5.24 中的部署轨迹由定义训练目标的同一 P_n 支配（通过更多算力而非更多数据接近），经典的训练到部署泛化问题变成了一个优化问题：最小化训练目标 J(Q)，使 Q 匹配 P_n。P_n 本身是否充分识别相关危害——即有限数据集是否包含足够信息供后验正确评估它们——是一个独立的、可信的问题，我们的定理未解决。另一个独立的问题是，先验 P_0 或数据收集过程本身可能被设计成使得后验目标 P_n 将部署引向有害结果。几个相邻挑战也不在范围内：对抗性查询和提示注入（尽管第 6 节讨论了作为未来工作的结构性防御）、基于 SAI 预测器构建的智能体脚手架的安全性（Bengio 等人，2025b）、完整的潜在知识引出挑战（仅部分缓解，第 2.4 节），以及渐近准确性声明的有限样本和有限算力修正。当前保证也针对单个预测器陈述。多个 SAI 预测器交互部署下的安全属性是否组合留给未来工作。

items.en: The full safety story has four ingredients: (a) the algebraic bound of Thm. 5.24 (formally requiring only the no-large-enrichment condition, Req. 5.21); (b) the small fraction RshellR_{\rm shell} of bad predictors within every loss band—a structural property of the Predictor initialization distribution μ\mu and the honest objective JJ (Req. 3.34); (c) consequence-invariance, which forbids explicit consequence-dependent training signals (Req. 3.36) such as reinforcement learning towards future outcomes caused by the SAI predictions, while restricting deployment-time Scaffold choices to explicit, audited criteria (Req. 5.17); and (d) a neutral target-construction process—the prior P0P_{0} and dataset DnD_{n} should function as purely epistemic inputs to the formation of the posterior.
items.zh: 完整的安全故事有四个要素：（a）定理 5.24 的代数界限（形式上仅要求无大富集条件，需求 5.21）；（b）每个损失带内坏预测器的小比例 R_shell——预测器初始化分布μ和诚实目标 J（需求 3.34）的结构属性；（c）后果不变性，禁止显式的后果依赖训练信号（需求 3.36），例如针对 SAI 预测引起的未来结果的强化学习，同时将部署时脚手架选择限制为显式、审计的标准（需求 5.17）；以及（d）中立的目标构建过程——先验 P_0 和数据集 D_n 应作为后验形成的纯认知输入。

items.en: The Scientist AI framework rests on several design choices that may appear to conflict with practical requirements or raise natural theoretical objections. This section, supplemented by Appendix C, aims to informally argue for our framework and address the most important objections directly.
items.zh: 科学家 AI 框架基于若干设计选择，这些选择可能看似与实践要求相冲突或引发自然的理论质疑。本节（辅以附录 C）旨在非正式地为我们的框架辩护，并直接回应最重要的质疑。

items.en: We claim that approximating the Bayesian posterior over contextualized statements incentivizes both accuracy and honesty. A detailed defense of this claim is given in Sec. 3; here, we sketch the four main components of that argument before considering objections to each of these claims.
items.zh: 我们声称，对上下文化陈述的贝叶斯后验进行近似，既能激励准确性，也能激励诚实性。该主张的详细辩护见第 3 节；在此，我们概述该论证的四个主要组成部分，然后考虑对每个主张的质疑。

items.en: Component A1: Contextualization makes the learning problem well-posed. The SAI is trained only on an epistemically contextualized dataset (Req. 3.25), which syntactically separates factual observations (verified measurements, proofs, program executions) from communication acts (“source S asserted claim X”, with metadata). This method of turning possibly false information (“claim X”) into factual one (“source S asserted X”) yields a well-defined learning target, i.e., the Bayesian posterior over these contextualized variables. This prevents the Predictor from treating unverified or socially repeated claims as ground-truth facts.
items.zh: 组件 A1：上下文化使学习问题适定。SAI 仅在一个认知上下文化的数据集（要求 3.25）上训练，该数据集在语法上将事实观测（经过验证的测量、证明、程序执行）与通信行为（“来源 S 声称了主张 X”，附带元数据）分开。这种将可能虚假的信息（“主张 X”）转化为事实信息（“来源 S 声称了 X”）的方法，产生了一个定义明确的学习目标，即这些上下文化变量的贝叶斯后验。这防止了预测器将未经验证或社会性重复的主张视为事实真相。

items.en: Component A2: Latent factual variables give training gradients that favor factual explanation over imitation. Most real-world data, especially outside of formal science, are best interpreted as communication acts: we may observe that a claim was made, but we do not know if the claim itself is true. To avoid training a system that collapses into imitation of what humans say, the SAI design introduces latent variable-statements written in factual syntax, corresponding to the truth of underlying claims (Def. 4.3 and Req. 4.6); for example, “XX is true” is forced to be one of the latent variables for the observed communication act “Author AA wrote XX”, in addition to any other explanatory latent variable that the Explainer generates. This avoids optimizing the SAI to reproduce the distribution of human continuations, including text reflecting goals, strategy, deception, or self-preservation. It also lets one latent fact explain communication acts across sources through a shared causal mechanism, favoring causal explanation over imitation (Assumptions 4.10 and 4.11).
items.zh: 组件 A2：潜在事实变量提供的训练梯度有利于事实解释而非模仿。大多数真实世界数据，尤其是形式科学之外的数据，最好被解释为通信行为：我们可能观察到某个主张被提出，但不知道主张本身是否为真。为了避免训练出崩溃为模仿人类话语的系统，SAI 设计引入了以事实语法编写的潜在变量陈述，对应于底层主张的真值（定义 4.3 和要求 4.6）；例如，对于观测到的通信行为“作者 AA 写了 XX”，除了解释器生成的任何其他解释性潜在变量外，“XX 为真”被强制作为潜在变量之一。这避免了优化 SAI 以再现人类续写的分布，包括反映目标、策略、欺骗或自我保存的文本。它还允许一个潜在事实通过共享的因果机制解释跨来源的通信行为，从而有利于因果解释而非模仿（假设 4.10 和 4.11）。

items.en: Component A3: The Predictor’s probability assignments over uncertain statements converge toward the Bayesian posterior. Even with unlimited informative data, most interesting statements are not perfectly deducible as True or False from the laws of mathematics. However, the Bayesian posterior probability of such latent statement-variables is a deterministic function of the data. However, the estimator QQ would generally not be certain about these values, and the entropy of QQ on such requests quantifies that uncertainty due to insufficient compute. With enough computation, QQ converges to PnP_{n} on observable statements (Prop. 4.16) and correctly estimates whether posterior probabilities lie inside or outside specified intervals for unobservable ones (Claim 4.18).11 1 This supports threshold and interval decisions, such as guardrail risk checks, using Q⁡(y∣x)Q(y\mid x) in place of Pn​(y∣x)P_{n}(y\mid x), with conservative estimated margins being chosen in terms of approximation error.
items.zh: 组件 A3：预测器对不确定陈述的概率分配收敛于贝叶斯后验。即使拥有无限的信息数据，大多数有趣的陈述也无法从数学定律中完美推导为真或假。然而，这些潜在陈述变量的贝叶斯后验概率是数据的确定性函数。但估计器 QQ 通常不会对这些值确定，QQ 在这些请求上的熵量化了因算力不足导致的不确定性。在足够计算量下，QQ 在可观测陈述上收敛于 Pn（命题 4.16），并正确估计不可观测陈述的后验概率是否位于指定区间内或外（主张 4.18）。11 1 这支持使用 Q(y|x)代替 Pn(y|x)进行阈值和区间决策（如护栏风险检查），并基于近似误差选择保守的估计裕度。

items.en: Component A4: Confident-but-miscalibrated predictions become vanishingly rare. Epistemic honesty involves a tradeoff between false positives and false negatives; thus, we should be concerned about measures of epistemic fidelity beyond simply average calibration. One particularly concerning failure mode of interest for this paper is the problem of confident falsehoods. With enough computation, the probability of encountering a query where QQ is highly confident but PnP_{n} is not vanishes (Prop. 4.14), yielding a formally accurate Predictor (Claim 4.19).
items.zh: 组件 A4：自信但校准不良的预测变得极其罕见。认知诚实涉及假阳性和假阴性之间的权衡；因此，我们应关注超出平均校准的认知保真度度量。本文特别关注的一个失败模式是自信虚假陈述问题。在足够计算量下，遇到 QQ 高度自信而 Pn 不自信的查询的概率消失（命题 4.14），从而产生形式上准确的预测器（主张 4.19）。

items.en: One may think that the very procedure we use to make the learning problem well-defined assumes controversial philosophical commitments. Our contextualization procedure (so the argument may go) must assume either that contested empirical or normative claims have a determinate truth-value, or assume views which state that contested normative or empirical claims cannot be evaluated as ‘true’ or ‘false’.22 2 The latter views are called ‘non-cognitivist’ views in the philosophical literature, and are themselves controversial (see Bourget and Chalmers 2023).
items.zh: 有人可能认为，我们用于使学习问题适定的程序本身假设了有争议的哲学承诺。我们的上下文化程序（可能如此论证）必须假设要么有争议的经验或规范性主张具有确定的真值，要么认为有争议的规范性或经验主张不能被评估为“真”或“假”的观点。22 2 后一种观点在哲学文献中被称为“非认知主义”观点，其本身也存在争议（见 Bourget and Chalmers 2023）。

items.en: Reply. Our epistemic contextualization pipeline (Def. 3.22) allows us to represent contested claims as communication acts—statements of the form “agent A asserts xx” that have determinate truth values regardless of the metaphysical status of xx itself. Consider a sentence like “Hello!” which is not a claim that is capable of being true or false. The contextualization pipeline translates such outputs into the form of communication acts (e.g., “Yoshua Bengio said ‘Hello!’ ”), allowing the model to learn from such outputs without being incentivized to emulate them. Other sentences may have the surface grammar of claims about the world (for instance, “Red is the best color”) while they really express preferences, so we would translate them similarly (“Yoshua Bengio said ‘Red is the best color’ ”). However, this does not import controversial philosophical commitments, as everyone agrees that statements of the form “Agent AA said ‘torture is wrong’ ” themselves have determinate truth-values. To the extent that one does believe that contested statements have determinate truth-values, the Scientist AI (much like human scientists themselves) has no guarantee of convergence to the moral truth. Our SAI accuracy argument should thus be viewed as providing guarantees for such determinate claims only.
items.zh: 回应。我们的认知上下文化流程（定义 3.22）允许我们将有争议的主张表示为通信行为——形式为“智能体 A 断言 x”的陈述，无论 x 本身的形而上学状态如何，这些陈述都具有确定的真值。考虑像“你好！”这样的句子，它不是一个能够为真或假的主张。上下文化流程将此类输出转化为通信行为的形式（例如，“Yoshua Bengio 说了‘你好！’”），使模型能够从这些输出中学习，而不被激励去模仿它们。其他句子可能具有关于世界的声称的表面语法（例如，“红色是最好的颜色”），而实际上表达的是偏好，因此我们类似地将其翻译（“Yoshua Bengio 说了‘红色是最好的颜色’”）。然而，这并未引入有争议的哲学承诺，因为每个人都同意“智能体 AA 说了‘酷刑是错误的’”这种形式的陈述本身具有确定的真值。就某人确实相信有争议的陈述具有确定真值而言，科学家 AI（很像人类科学家本身）无法保证收敛于道德真理。因此，我们的 SAI 准确性论证应被视为仅为这类确定的主张提供保证。

items.en: Even putting philosophical issues to one side, one may worry that the vast majority of training data—at least outside of mathematics and formal science—consists predominantly of communication acts. Hence, one may be concerned that factual syntax (i.e., the syntax through which the SAI is queried for honest predictions) may fail to generalize to domains where it was rarely encountered during training.
items.zh: 即使将哲学问题放在一边，人们可能担心绝大多数训练数据——至少在数学和形式科学之外——主要由通信行为组成。因此，人们可能担心事实语法（即 SAI 被查询以进行诚实预测所使用的语法）可能无法泛化到训练中很少遇到的领域。

items.en: Reply. The design addresses this through Req. 4.6: the Explainer must include the factual truth variable “xx is true” as a latent explanation whenever a communication act asserts xx. Even in domains with no directly observed factual statements, the Predictor thereby receives training gradients—via the latent variable model—that exercise the factual syntax and force it to assign a posterior probability to the underlying claim. DnD_{n} will also contain scientific data proper (e.g., observations from medical studies, replicated experimental measurements, formally verified proofs, etc.), which provide a direct training signal for the factual mode. At the same time, communicated scientific theories are treated as communication acts; the SAI can treat them as useful latent hypotheses without being constrained to accept the current scientific consensus as ground truth, assigning posterior mass to competing theories in proportion to their consistency with directly observed evidence.
items.zh: 回应。该设计通过要求 4.6 解决了这个问题：每当通信行为断言 x 时，解释器必须包含事实真值变量“x 为真”作为潜在解释。即使在没有直接观测到事实陈述的领域，预测器也通过潜在变量模型接收到训练梯度，这些梯度训练了事实语法并迫使其为底层主张分配后验概率。Dn 还将包含适当的科学数据（例如，来自医学研究的观测、可重复的实验测量、形式验证的证明等），这些数据为事实模式提供了直接的训练信号。同时，被传达的科学理论被视为通信行为；SAI 可以将它们视为有用的潜在假设，而不必被约束接受当前科学共识为事实真相，而是根据与直接观测证据的一致性，将后验质量分配给竞争理论。

items.en: Our framework assumes that factual latents will tend to dominate the posterior because they provide compressive causal explanations (Schölkopf et al. 2021) which are unified across contexts (Assumption 4.10); the compositional structure of natural language further supports the generalization of this distinction to unseen domains (Assumption 4.11). These claims are best viewed as empirical adequacy premises for (rather than proven consequences of) the present framework, insofar as they specify what must be validated for factual syntax to remain useful outside domains with direct factual supervision. A separate objection is that exact Bayesian inference is intractable and the available data are finite. We treat the formal accuracy claims in this paper as asymptotic in data and computation, with approximation error handled through conservative margins and abstention when uncertainty is large; see Appendix C.2.
items.zh: 我们的框架假设事实潜在变量将倾向于主导后验，因为它们提供了跨上下文统一的压缩因果解释（Schölkopf et al. 2021）；自然语言的组合结构进一步支持这种区分向未见领域的泛化（假设 4.11）。这些主张最好被视为当前框架的经验充分性前提（而非已证明的结论），因为它们指定了必须验证什么才能使事实语法在直接事实监督领域之外保持有用。另一个反对意见是精确贝叶斯推理难以处理，且可用数据有限。我们将本文中的形式准确性主张视为数据和计算上的渐近结果，近似误差通过保守裕度和不确定性较大时的弃权来处理；见附录 C.2。

items.en: After the arguments for the accuracy of the Predictor, we turn to arguments for its safety. The SAI design explicitly separates deployment-time decisions (which may explicitly veto or rank candidate outputs on safety grounds) from the Predictor’s training process (which explicitly prohibits any feedback loop rewarding the Predictor for the downstream consequences of its deployed predictions).
items.zh: 在论证了预测器的准确性之后，我们转向其安全性的论证。SAI 设计明确将部署时的决策（可能基于安全理由明确否决或排序候选输出）与预测器的训练过程（明确禁止任何奖励预测器因其部署预测的下游后果的反馈循环）分开。

items.en: Although it is difficult to be sure of intention, it has a statistically recognizable behavioral signature: reliably producing an outcome that unaimed behavior would reach only with vanishing probability. Such an outcome is a “needle in a haystack”: reliably finding it requires optimization guided by a signal of proximity, and the SAI design withholds such a signal from Predictor training. The safety argument has the following three components.
items.zh: 虽然难以确定意图，但它有一个统计上可识别的行为特征：可靠地产生一个无目标行为仅以极小概率达到的结果。这样的结果是“大海捞针”：可靠地找到它需要由接近信号引导的优化，而 SAI 设计对预测器训练扣留了这样的信号。安全性论证包含以下三个组成部分。

items.en: Component S1: Coordinated action over time is required to pursue difficult and harmful goals. We consider harms HH that are unlikely to result from a single mistaken prediction from the Predictor, that either require many Guardrail deviations of QQ from PnP_{n} or that those deviations are very large. To produce such highly dangerous outputs, the Predictor must issue many false-negative safety judgments—each consistent with the others and with whatever cover story explains them in deployment—sustained across many queries or many users. Using the formalism of this paper, the pursuit of a harmful goal requires that the guardrailed Predictor’s harm estimate falls below a threshold α\alpha on each set of safety-critical queries that together enable HH.
items.zh: 组件 S1：追求困难且有害的目标需要随时间协调的行动。我们考虑危害 H，它不太可能由预测器的单个错误预测导致，而是需要许多 Q 偏离 Pn 的护栏违规，或者这些偏离非常大。要产生如此高度危险的输出，预测器必须发出许多假阴性安全判断——每个判断彼此一致，并与部署中解释它们的任何掩护故事一致——并在许多查询或许多用户之间持续。使用本文的形式化，追求有害目标要求被护栏保护的预测器的危害估计在每组实现 H 所需的安全关键查询上低于阈值α。

items.en: Component S2: Dangerous Predictors are rare at every level of loss. Under the unstructured initialization distribution over Predictors μ\mu, only a small fraction of Predictors produce outputs that keep such a harmful plan viable; it would be highly surprising if a randomly initialized Predictor could achieve a complex, dangerous goal. The stronger claim we need is that this remains true after conditioning on any fixed loss level. Letting Rshell​(H,Asafe)R_{\rm shell}(H,A_{\rm safe}) denote the largest fraction of dangerous Predictors found inside any loss band under μ\mu, we claim RshellR_{\rm shell} is structurally small.
items.zh: 组件 S2：在每个损失水平上，危险预测器都很罕见。在预测器的非结构化初始化分布μ下，只有一小部分预测器产生的输出能使这样的有害计划可行；随机初始化的预测器能够实现复杂危险目标将是高度令人惊讶的。我们需要的更强主张是，在条件于任何固定损失水平后，这一点仍然成立。令 Rshell(H, Asafe)表示在μ下任何损失带内危险预测器的最大比例，我们声称 Rshell 在结构上是小的。

items.en:
Component S3: Training does not concentrate probability mass on the dangerous subset. Instead of thinking about the randomness in the actions of a given Predictor, we consider the distribution of Predictors resulting from the training process ν
u, and how likely they are to cause HH. Even if dangerous Predictors are initially rare within each loss band, we still need to control whether training concentrates probability mass on that subset. Because ν
u is consequence-invariant, it carries no direct consequence-dependent signal selecting the dangerous subset. The training process ν
u can still reweight Predictors through ordinary optimizer-visible properties based on the training objective; the safety question is whether those properties accidentally localize dangerous Predictors within a loss band. Hence, the argument requires that such accidental enrichment remain bounded. This yields the paper’s central safety result (Thm. 5.24): writing νt
u_{t} for the marginal of the training process ν
u at step tt, then for any harmful event HH and safety threshold AsafeA_{m safe} at which Req. 5.21 holds, and uniformly in training step tt, the probability of obtaining a dangerous Predictor is bounded by:
items.zh: 组件 S3：训练不会将概率质量集中在危险子集上。我们不思考给定预测器行为的随机性，而是考虑训练过程ν产生的预测器分布，以及它们导致 H 的可能性。即使危险预测器在每个损失带内最初很罕见，我们仍需控制训练是否将概率质量集中在该子集上。由于ν是后果不变的，它不携带直接依赖于后果的信号来选择危险子集。训练过程ν仍然可以通过基于训练目标的普通优化器可见属性重新加权预测器；安全性问题是这些属性是否偶然地将危险预测器定位在损失带内。因此，论证要求这种偶然富集保持有界。这产生了本文的核心安全性结果（定理 5.24）：记νt 为训练过程ν在步骤 t 的边缘分布，则对于任何有害事件 H 和满足要求 5.21 的安全阈值 Asafe，且一致于训练步骤 t，获得危险预测器的概率有界于：

items.tex: \nu_{t}\{Q:Q\text{ is dangerous}\}\leq C_{\rm bad}(H,A_{\rm safe})\,R_{\rm shell}(H,A_{\rm safe}),

items.en: where Rshell​(H,Asafe)R_{\rm shell}(H,A_{\rm safe}) is the largest fraction of dangerous Predictors found inside any loss band under μ\mu, and Cbad​(H,Asafe)C_{\rm bad}(H,A_{\rm safe}) denotes how much the chosen noisy training process can increase this fraction within a band. If the degree to which the noisy training process can increase this fraction is small (i.e., if CbadC_{\rm bad} is small), then the probability of obtaining a dangerous Predictor is small. We argue that CbadC_{\rm bad} is likely to be small (Remark 5.22) because of consequence-invariance, and thus our bound delivers an optimistic safety result on the probability of obtaining a dangerous Predictor.
items.zh: 其中 Rshell(H, Asafe)是μ下任何损失带内危险预测器的最大比例，Cbad(H, Asafe)表示所选带噪训练过程能在带内增加该比例的程度。如果带噪训练过程增加该比例的程度很小（即 Cbad 很小），那么获得危险预测器的概率很小。我们论证 Cbad 很可能是小的（注记 5.22），因为后果不变性，因此我们的界给出了关于获得危险预测器概率的乐观安全性结果。

items.en: A natural worry is that a sufficiently capable system trained to minimize prediction error might learn to influence the world it is meant to model. If post-deployment outcomes are used as a training signal, selecting checkpoints or outputs based on downstream error would create optimization pressure to steer the world. More broadly, one might think that any sufficiently capable system with a persistent goal tends to develop instrumental subgoals such as self-preservation and resource acquisition (Omohundro 2018; Russell 2019)—the prediction-accuracy goal is no exception.
items.zh: 一个自然的担忧是，一个足够有能力的系统，如果被训练以最小化预测误差，可能会学会影响它旨在建模的世界。如果部署后的结果被用作训练信号，基于下游误差选择检查点或输出将产生优化压力以操纵世界。更广泛地说，人们可能认为任何具有持久目标的足够有能力的系统都会发展出工具性子目标，如自我保存和资源获取（Omohundro 2018; Russell 2019）——预测准确性目标也不例外。

items.en: Reply. The SAI design requires the Predictor’s training process ν\nu to satisfy the formal core of consequence-invariant training (Req. 3.36), which rules out explicit algorithmic signals that reward, penalize, rank, or select candidate Predictors because of the realized or desired downstream consequences of deploying them. Because future outcomes may enter JJ as variables to be predicted, this process still allows the Predictor to make predictions about the consequences of given actions. By design, consequence-invariance thus rules out any reinforcement-learning-style loop in which the Predictor is selected because it would lead to preferred real-world effects, as well as subtler forms of feedback, such as choosing among checkpoints based on downstream error measured after deployment.
items.zh: 回应。SAI 设计要求预测器的训练过程ν满足后果不变训练的形式核心（要求 3.36），这排除了因部署预测器的已实现或期望的下游后果而奖励、惩罚、排序或选择候选预测器的显式算法信号。由于未来结果可能作为待预测变量进入 J，该过程仍然允许预测器对给定行动的后果进行预测。通过设计，后果不变性因此排除了任何强化学习风格的循环，其中预测器因其会导致偏好的现实世界效果而被选择，以及更微妙的反馈形式，如基于部署后测量的下游误差在检查点之间进行选择。

items.en: Concretely, post-deployment consequences may be queried via interventional predictives (Ortega et al. 2021). These consequences, however, are not fed back into the current training run as an on-policy signal: they are not appended to DnD_{n}, nor does any training signal select the current Predictor for the downstream effects of its own deployment. If such consequences are later observed, they may be recorded only as ordinary contextualized observations in a future enlarged dataset Dn′⊇DnD_{n^{\prime}}\supseteq D_{n} for a subsequent training run. Likewise, checkpoint selection depends only on fixed-data metrics derived from DnD_{n}. At deployment time, the Scaffold may still reason about downstream consequences, but only through explicit, audited criteria—in this paper, safety results primarily through the Guardrail and, when appropriate, safer tie-breaking among candidate performative fixed points, all under Req. 5.17. The statelessness of QQ (Def. 3.19) provides a further mitigation against strategies that require a persistent state across queries. Predicting downstream consequences does not require optimizing the Predictor for them: QQ can model agents and accurately forecast the effects of its own deployed outputs while still producing an epistemically honest posterior estimate (formalized in Sec. 4).
items.zh: 具体来说，部署后的后果可以通过干预性预测进行查询（Ortega et al. 2021）。然而，这些后果不会作为在策略信号反馈到当前训练运行中：它们不会被附加到 Dn，也没有任何训练信号因当前预测器自身部署的下游效应而选择它。如果这些后果后来被观测到，它们可能仅作为普通上下文化观测记录在未来的扩大数据集 Dn'⊇Dn 中，用于后续训练运行。同样，检查点选择仅依赖于从 Dn 导出的固定数据指标。在部署时，脚手架可能仍然推理下游后果，但仅通过明确的、可审计的标准——在本文中，安全性主要通过护栏以及在适当时在候选执行固定点之间进行更安全的打破平局来实现，所有这些都在要求 5.17 下。Q 的无状态性（定义 3.19）提供了对需要跨查询持久状态的策略的进一步缓解。预测下游后果不需要为此优化预测器：Q 可以建模智能体并准确预测其自身部署输出的效果，同时仍然产生认知诚实的后验估计（在第 4 节中形式化）。

items.en: Even under consequence-invariant training, one might ask whether the randomness of training could accidentally produce a dangerous Predictor, or whether an emergent inner optimizer (Hubinger et al. 2019) could generalize in goal-directed ways unrewarded by the outer objective.
items.zh: 即使在后果不变训练下，人们可能会问，训练的随机性是否可能意外地产生一个危险的预测器，或者一个涌现的内部优化器（Hubinger 等人，2019）是否可能以外部目标未奖励的目标导向方式泛化。

items.en: Reply. At the exact loss minimum, Req. 3.34 gives Q=PnQ=P_{n}, and Prop. 5.3 shows that this honest target is consequence-invariant. Under the “guardrail-adequacy” condition, aH​(Pn)≤Asafea_{H}(P_{n})\leq A_{\rm safe}, its guarded deployment is safe. The non-asymptotic question is whether training can pass through dangerous positive-loss Predictors before reaching that endpoint.
items.zh: 回答。在精确损失最小值处，要求 3.34 给出 Q=PnQ=P_{n}，命题 5.3 表明这个诚实的预测目标是后果不变的。在“护栏充分性”条件下，aH​(Pn)≤Asafea_{H}(P_{n})\leq A_{\rm safe}，其受防护部署是安全的。非渐近问题是，训练在到达该终点之前是否会经过危险的正常损失预测器。

items.en: The answer proposed here relies on the levels of loss. Regardless of whether the offending outputs arise from an inner optimizer, an ordinary approximation error, or a posterior explaining the data through a hidden strategic actor, sustaining HH requires a structured pattern of Guardrail-relevant deviations. Within a fixed loss level, or band, this pattern is argued to be geometrically sparse. Any remaining enrichment must therefore arise through accidental correlation with training-visible features, quantified by CbadC_{\rm bad} and refined by KB​(t)K_{B}(t) in Appendix B. Attempts to manipulate the collection of future data are unrewarded by the current training signal because the current run trains on the fixed dataset DnD_{n}.
items.zh: 这里提出的答案依赖于损失水平。无论违规输出来自内部优化器、普通近似误差，还是通过隐藏的战略行动者解释数据的后验，维持 HH 都需要一个结构化的护栏相关偏差模式。在一个固定的损失水平或带内，这种模式被认为在几何上是稀疏的。因此，任何剩余的丰富化必须通过训练可见特征的偶然相关性产生，由 CbadC_{\rm bad} 量化，并由附录 B 中的 KB​(t)K_{B}(t) 细化。操纵未来数据收集的尝试不会受到当前训练信号的奖励，因为当前运行在固定数据集 DnD_{n} 上训练。

items.en: The following two objections concern the modeling setup shared by the accuracy and safety arguments, i.e., how deployed predictions and latent knowledge are represented.
items.zh: 以下两个反对意见涉及准确性和安全性论证共享的建模设置，即部署的预测和潜在知识如何表示。

items.en: If the deployed predictions of the SAI system affect the variables to be predicted by QQ, doesn’t the design risk rendering those deployed predictions incompatible with their effects?
items.zh: 如果 SAI 系统的部署预测影响 QQ 要预测的变量，那么该设计是否会使这些部署预测与其效果不兼容？

items.en: Reply. The SAI treats deployed predictions Q~\widetilde{Q} as intervened variables (see Req. 3.28). This means that the Predictor can model the consequences of deploying a prediction via a do(Q~=⋅)\mathrm{do}(\widetilde{Q}=\cdot) intervention (Ortega et al. 2021) without those consequences flowing back as a training signal, thereby yielding a unique, well-defined Bayesian posterior without any fixed-point requirement on the posterior target itself. In cases (such as, e.g., self-fulfilling prophecies) where multiple performatively consistent fixed points exist (Perdomo et al. 2020), the Scaffold selects among them using explicit, audited criteria (Req. 5.17). If none exists, then the Scaffold can select a prediction which approximately minimizes the residual from the performative prediction constraint (Def. 3.41). Performativity is therefore a reason to be careful about how Q~\widetilde{Q} is formed from QQ—but it is not, by itself, a reason for the Predictor to seek implicit unaudited goals.
items.zh: 回答。SAI 将部署预测 Q~\widetilde{Q} 视为干预变量（见要求 3.28）。这意味着预测器可以通过 do(Q~=⋅)\mathrm{do}(\widetilde{Q}=\cdot) 干预（Ortega 等人，2021）对部署预测的后果进行建模，而无需这些后果作为训练信号回流，从而产生唯一、定义明确的贝叶斯后验，而无需对后验目标本身有任何不动点要求。在存在多个执行一致不动点的情况下（例如，自我实现的预言）（Perdomo 等人，2020），Scaffold 使用明确的、经过审计的标准（要求 5.17）在其中进行选择。如果不存在，则 Scaffold 可以选择一个预测，该预测近似最小化执行预测约束的残差（定义 3.41）。因此，执行性是谨慎处理 Q~\widetilde{Q} 如何从 QQ 形成的一个原因——但它本身并不是预测器寻求隐式未经审计目标的原因。

items.en: The eliciting latent knowledge (ELK) challenge (Christiano et al. 2021) has been raised as a fundamental obstacle to any safety case grounded in AI honesty: if a sufficiently capable model’s outputs need not reflect its internal beliefs, then guarantees about output honesty provide no assurance about what the model “actually knows” or intends. The ELK challenge asks how to ensure that a model’s outputs faithfully reflect its internal beliefs, which can be difficult when the learner can only make interpretable predictions over the variables in the data.
items.zh: 引出潜在知识（ELK）挑战（Christiano 等人，2021）已被视为基于 AI 诚实性的任何安全案例的根本障碍：如果一个足够有能力的模型的输出不必反映其内部信念，那么关于输出诚实性的保证就无法提供关于模型“实际知道”或意图的任何保证。ELK 挑战询问如何确保模型的输出忠实地反映其内部信念，当学习器只能对数据中的变量进行可解释预测时，这可能很困难。

items.en: Reply. The SAI design partially mitigates one important aspect of this challenge by requiring latent-variable probabilistic reasoning to be expressed through explicit natural-language statements. Thanks to the compositional nature of language and the fact that the SAI uses the same language to name observed and latent variables, it can generalize to the semantics of queries that have never been seen in the training set (see Prop. 4.16). It is thus possible to query the SAI about its latent variables using factual syntax (communication act syntax would only tell us what a human would say), capturing the part of its internal beliefs that can be verbalized. The Explainer (Def. 4.3) must articulate which latent variables it relies on; the conditional probability of the truth values of those latent variables is produced by QQ, which acts as a verifier for each statement in the argument generated by the Explainer, and these probabilities feed directly into the final prediction. The latent statements generate gradients back into QQ during training (Req. 4.6), so the expressed reasoning must actually contribute to predictive accuracy. For any latent knowledge that the model can verbalize in natural language, an explicitly agentic explanation should appear in sampled Explainer traces and thus be auditable; conversely, a merely plausible-looking rationale that does not actually support its conclusion must violate the probabilistic consistency constraints among the stated latent variables’ probabilities, and is penalized by JJ. Unlike a System 1 scalar output, a System 2 false negative is a composed, gradient-bearing chain of QQ-evaluated probabilities, so it cannot be plausible at each step and yet harmful in its conclusion for free.
items.zh: 回答。SAI 设计通过要求潜在变量概率推理通过明确的自然语言陈述来表达，部分缓解了这一挑战的一个重要方面。由于语言的组合性质以及 SAI 使用相同的语言来命名观察变量和潜在变量，它可以泛化到训练集中从未见过的查询的语义（见命题 4.16）。因此，可以使用事实语法（通信行为语法只会告诉我们人类会说什么）查询 SAI 关于其潜在变量的信息，从而捕获其内部信念中可言语化的部分。解释器（定义 4.3）必须阐明其依赖的潜在变量；这些潜在变量真值的条件概率由 QQ 产生，它充当解释器生成的论证中每个陈述的验证器，并且这些概率直接输入到最终预测中。潜在陈述在训练期间将梯度传回 QQ（要求 4.6），因此表达出的推理必须实际有助于预测准确性。对于模型可以用自然语言言语化的任何潜在知识，显式的智能体式解释应出现在采样的解释器轨迹中，因此是可审计的；相反，一个看似合理但实际不支持其结论的理由必须违反所述潜在变量概率之间的概率一致性约束，并受到 JJ 的惩罚。与系统 1 标量输出不同，系统 2 假阴性是一个由 QQ 评估的概率组成的、承载梯度的链，因此它不可能在每一步都合理，却免费产生有害结论。

items.en: However, we acknowledge that ELK is not fully resolved. Non-verbal internal representations that cannot surface through the Explainer could, in principle, contain beliefs not expressible as SAI statements. The remaining concern is therefore an implementation-level inner optimizer in QQ’s weights that produces generally coherent explanations while steering safety-critical outputs. To be dangerous, such an optimizer must either surface agentic structure in auditable explanations, or sustain a coherent pattern of predictive deviations (relevant to Guardrail decisions); the latter is exactly the high-loss, coordinated behavior targeted by Theorems 5.24 and B.10. Thus, the safety guarantees we provide do not require ELK to be solved in full.
items.zh: 然而，我们承认 ELK 并未完全解决。无法通过解释器浮现的非言语内部表示原则上可能包含无法表示为 SAI 陈述的信念。因此，剩余的担忧是 QQ 权重中的实现级内部优化器，它在产生一般连贯解释的同时操纵安全关键输出。要变得危险，这样的优化器必须在可审计的解释中浮现智能体式结构，或者维持一个连贯的预测偏差模式（与护栏决策相关）；后者正是定理 5.24 和 B.10 针对的高损失、协调行为。因此，我们提供的安全保证不需要完全解决 ELK。

items.en: Exact Bayesian inference is typically intractable, and the data are finite, shifting, and sometimes adversarially curated. Why should we trust an approximate-posterior system, and how are safety-relevant errors controlled?
items.zh: 精确贝叶斯推理通常是难以处理的，并且数据是有限的、变化的，有时是敌对策划的。为什么我们应该信任一个近似后验系统，以及如何控制安全相关误差？

items.en: Reply. To the extent that one is concerned about the specifically safety-focused version of this worry (see Sec. C.2 for our reply to the accuracy-focused objection), one must be concerned that the SAI Predictor training procedure creates selection pressure toward downstream outcomes. Here, worries about approximation limitations may motivate conservatism, but they do not, by themselves, reintroduce a reward signal for steering the world. Our key safety result (Thm. 5.24) requires only that: (i) Predictors capable of sustaining harmful trajectories incur the corresponding loss threshold, and (ii) that stochastic training does not substantially enrich such dangerous Predictors within the loss bands it visits. As such, approximation errors then appear as elevated loss or uncertainty. While safety-critical deployment may still require conservative margins and abstention when approximation error is large, the constraints of our setup seem to preempt the emergence of hidden objectives aiming to steer towards downstream outcomes.
items.zh: 回答。就人们特别关注安全焦点版本的这一担忧（见附录 C.2 中我们对准确性焦点反对意见的回复）而言，必须担心 SAI 预测器训练过程会向下游结果产生选择压力。在这里，对近似局限性的担忧可能激发保守主义，但它们本身并不会重新引入操纵世界的奖励信号。我们的关键安全结果（定理 5.24）仅要求：（i）能够维持有害轨迹的预测器会招致相应的损失阈值，以及（ii）随机训练不会在其访问的损失带内显著丰富此类危险预测器。因此，近似误差随后表现为高损失或不确定性。虽然安全关键部署可能仍需要保守裕度和在近似误差较大时弃权，但我们的设置约束似乎阻止了旨在操纵下游结果的隐藏目标的出现。

full.sec: 3 Forming a Probabilistic Model of the World

items.en: Our approach is to approximate an extremely expressive Bayesian posterior. But over what, exactly? Bayesian methods rely heavily on the foundational modeling assumptions, so we take care in this section to set up the underlying probability space properly—even though, in practice, the predictor QQ will never exactly represent a consistent probability distribution over that space. We aim to benefit from the expressivity of natural and artificial languages. However, the fact that the predictor is itself a part of the world poses certain modeling difficulties (Demski and Garrabrant 2020; Meulemans et al. 2025), which we must take care to handle deftly as well.
items.zh: 我们的方法是近似一个极其表达性的贝叶斯后验。但具体是关于什么的？贝叶斯方法严重依赖于基础建模假设，因此我们在本节中小心地正确设置底层概率空间——尽管在实践中，预测器 QQ 永远不会精确表示该空间上的一个一致概率分布。我们旨在受益于自然语言和人工语言的表达性。然而，预测器本身是世界的一部分这一事实带来了某些建模困难（Demski 和 Garrabrant，2020；Meulemans 等人，2025），我们也必须巧妙地处理这些困难。

items.en: While our underlying model in the next few subsections has been fully defined, the remainder of the paper contains a mix of formal and informal content. The formal results—along with those Definitions and Requirements that are stated in fully formal terms—follow deductively from the stated definitions and requirements. The informal content rests on empirical considerations and conceptual insights that we cannot yet fully formalize. We reserve the labels Theorem, Proposition, Proof, and Corollary for the former, and Claim, Argument, and Assumption for the latter.
items.zh: 虽然我们在接下来几个小节中的基础模型已完全定义，但论文的其余部分包含正式和非正式内容的混合。正式结果——以及那些以完全正式术语陈述的定义和要求——从所述定义和要求中演绎得出。非正式内容依赖于我们尚无法完全形式化的经验考虑和概念见解。我们将定理、命题、证明和推论标签保留给前者，将声明、论证和假设标签保留给后者。

full.sec: 3.1 Modeling tools: causal models, statements, datasets, and targets
full.secZh: 3.1 建模工具：因果模型、陈述、数据集与目标

items.en: We begin with a (countable) collection of statements in natural language, mathematics, or code—each of which describes a determinate property of the world. We call this set of basic semantically meaningful strings statements, denoting the set of all such statements by 𝒮\mathcal{S}. For each statement s∈𝒮s\in\mathcal{S}, we suppose there is a corresponding Boolean variable vs∈{0,1}v_{s}\in\{0,1\} called a statement-variable, where vs=1v_{s}=1 is to be interpreted as the assertion expressed by ss being true. Not all strings should be thought of as valid statements.
items.zh: 我们从自然语言、数学或代码中的（可数）陈述集合开始——每个陈述描述世界的一个确定属性。我们将这个基本语义有意义的字符串集合称为陈述，用 𝒮\mathcal{S} 表示所有此类陈述的集合。对于每个陈述 s∈𝒮s\in\mathcal{S}，我们假设存在一个对应的布尔变量 vs∈{0,1}v_{s}\in\{0,1\}，称为陈述变量，其中 vs=1v_{s}=1 被解释为 ss 表达的断言为真。并非所有字符串都应被视为有效陈述。

items.en: “It rains in Montreal on January 1st, 2030” could be an example of a valid statement, i.e., it is the name of a variable. At the time of this writing, its truth value is unknown. Examples of invalid statements include “Hello!” and “Red is the best color”. An invalid statement can often be turned into a valid one by adding context, e.g., “Yoshua uttered Hello!” and “Yoshua prefers the color red over others”.
items.zh: “2030 年 1 月 1 日蒙特利尔下雨”可能是一个有效陈述的例子，即它是一个变量的名称。在撰写本文时，其真值未知。无效陈述的例子包括“你好！”和“红色是最好的颜色”。无效陈述通常可以通过添加上下文变为有效，例如“Yoshua 说了你好！”和“Yoshua 更喜欢红色而不是其他颜色”。

items.en: The raw training data can contain invalid statements, but they are transformed into valid ones by the automated epistemic contextualization process (formalized in Def. 3.22). In practice, most statements will be recorded as communication acts (attributed to a speaker in a specific context), while some scientific statements may be assigned a factual meaning by the designers of the contextualization pipeline.
items.zh: 原始训练数据可能包含无效陈述，但它们通过自动化的认知语境化过程（形式化于定义 3.22）被转换为有效陈述。在实践中，大多数陈述将被记录为通信行为（归因于特定语境中的说话者），而一些科学陈述可能由语境化流程的设计者赋予事实含义。

items.en: The variables of our system that we most care about are those indexed by these statements, but we cannot stop there: we need explicit variables (Q~\widetilde{Q}) encoding the effect of deploying a predictor. This creates a knot: anything describable in terms of any variables is a potential query and hence leads to a new deployment variable that must also be part of the basic variables of our causal model. So we turn to the classical trick for handling cyclic definitions: inductively defining all syntactic objects at once. Bear with us a moment.
items.zh: 我们系统中最关心的变量是由这些陈述索引的变量，但我们不能止步于此：我们需要显式变量（Q~\widetilde{Q}）来编码部署预测器的效果。这造成了一个难题：任何可以用任何变量描述的东西都是一个潜在的查询，因此导致一个新的部署变量，该变量也必须成为我们因果模型的基本变量的一部分。因此，我们转向处理循环定义的经典技巧：同时归纳定义所有语法对象。请稍等片刻。

items.en: Let 𝒮0\mathcal{S}_{0} be a countable set of valid statements, including communication acts. Fix a bit precision BB for the predictor. For k=1,2,…k=1,2,\ldots, recursively define
items.zh: 设 𝒮0\mathcal{S}_{0} 是一个可数的有效陈述集合，包括通信行为。固定预测器的比特精度 BB。对于 k=1,2,…k=1,2,\ldots，递归定义

items.en: Then, define 𝒮:=⋃k≥0𝒮k\mathcal{S}:=\bigcup_{k\geq 0}\mathcal{S}_{k} and ℒ:=⋃k≥0ℒk\mathcal{L}:=\bigcup_{k\geq 0}\mathcal{L}_{k}.
items.zh: 然后，定义 𝒮:=⋃k≥0𝒮k\mathcal{S}:=\bigcup_{k\geq 0}\mathcal{S}_{k} 和 ℒ:=⋃k≥0ℒk\mathcal{L}:=\bigcup_{k\geq 0}\mathcal{L}_{k}。

items.en: Intuitively, ℒ\mathcal{L} is the language (that of a Boolean algebra for queries) over the truth values of the statement-variables indexed by 𝒮\mathcal{S}. The new statements added to each level of 𝒮\mathcal{S} comprise, for each pair (x,y)∈ℒ×ℒ(x,y)\in\mathcal{L}\times\mathcal{L} and n∈ℕn\in\mathbb{N}, the precision-BB binary representation of a rational number Q~n​(y∣x)∈[0,1]\widetilde{Q}_{n}(y\mid x)\in[0,1]. (In this notation, q.bq.b indicates the bb-th bit in the binary representation of qq.) These numbers will later be interpreted as predictor deployments (Sec. 3.3) and are central to making a predictor insensitive to the consequences of deployment—but we defer this. In the end, 𝒮\mathcal{S} and ℒ\mathcal{L} remain countable sets of statements, their elements representable as strings of tokens.
items.zh: 直观上，ℒ\mathcal{L} 是由 𝒮\mathcal{S} 索引的陈述变量真值上的语言（用于查询的布尔代数）。添加到 𝒮\mathcal{S} 每一层的新陈述包括，对于每对 (x,y)∈ℒ×ℒ(x,y)\in\mathcal{L}\times\mathcal{L} 和 n∈ℕn\in\mathbb{N}，精度为 BB 的有理数 Q~n​(y∣x)∈[0,1]\widetilde{Q}_{n}(y\mid x)\in[0,1] 的二进制表示。（在此表示法中，q.bq.b 表示 qq 的二进制表示中的第 bb 位。）这些数字稍后将被解释为预测器部署（第 3.3 节），并且对于使预测器对部署后果不敏感至关重要——但我们推迟这一点。最终，𝒮\mathcal{S} 和 ℒ\mathcal{L} 仍然是可数的陈述集合，其元素可表示为令牌字符串。

items.en: Let 𝒱:={vs:s∈𝒮}\mathcal{V}:=\{v_{s}:s\in\mathcal{S}\} be the endogenous (Boolean) variables of the model.
items.zh: 设 𝒱:={vs:s∈𝒮}\mathcal{V}:=\{v_{s}:s\in\mathcal{S}\} 为模型的内生（布尔）变量。

items.en: In addition to the variables 𝒱\cal V, we also need to model the data-collection process, so that we can properly condition on the dataset. We model these variables as distinct and causally exogenous.
items.zh: 除变量 𝒱\cal V 外，我们还需要对数据收集过程进行建模，以便能够恰当地以数据集为条件。我们将这些变量建模为不同的且因果外生的变量。

items.en: An observation is a statement-variable observed to take value 11. The dataset Dn=(x1,…,xn)D_{n}=(x_{1},\ldots,x_{n}) is a sequence of such observations. Let XiX_{i} be a new kind of variable (taking values in 𝒮\mathcal{S}), indicating the identity of the statement selected for the ii-th observation, so that vXi=1v_{X_{i}}=1 is the observed event. We write 𝒳:={Xi}i=1∞\mathcal{X}:=\{X_{i}\}_{i=1}^{\infty} for the indexed collection of selection variables.
items.zh: 一个观测是一个被观察到取值为 11 的陈述变量。数据集 Dn=(x1,…,xn)D_{n}=(x_{1},\ldots,x_{n}) 是这样一个观测序列。令 XiX_{i} 为一种新变量（取值于 𝒮\mathcal{S}），表示第 ii 次观测所选陈述的标识，因此 vXi=1v_{X_{i}}=1 是被观测的事件。我们记 𝒳:={Xi}i=1∞\mathcal{X}:=\{X_{i}\}_{i=1}^{\infty} 为选择变量的索引集合。

items.en: Although DnD_{n} is formally a sequence (x1,…,xn)(x_{1},\ldots,x_{n}) of statements, we freely identify it with the event ⋀i=1n(Xi=xi∧vXi=1)\bigwedge_{i=1}^{n}(X_{i}=x_{i}\land v_{X_{i}}=1) when it appears as a conditioning argument in a probability expression. This is equivalent to conditioning on the event X1:n=DnX_{1:n}=D_{n}, under the assumption that only true things are observed (i.e., ∃i.Xi=x⟹vx=1\exists i.~X_{i}=x\implies v_{x}=1), as is ensured by contextualization (Def. 3.22).
items.zh: 尽管 DnD_{n} 形式上是一个陈述序列 (x1,…,xn)(x_{1},\ldots,x_{n})，但当它作为概率表达式中的条件参数时，我们将其自由地等同于事件 ⋀i=1n(Xi=xi∧vXi=1)\bigwedge_{i=1}^{n}(X_{i}=x_{i}\land v_{X_{i}}=1)。这等价于以事件 X1:n=DnX_{1:n}=D_{n} 为条件，并假设只观测到真实事物（即 ∃i. Xi=x⟹vx=1\exists i.~X_{i}=x\implies v_{x}=1），这由语境化（定义 3.22）保证。

items.en: Our framework also requires a formalization of causality. For simplicity, we build on finite structural causal models (SCMs) (Pearl 2009). 33 3 Given the expressivity of our model, we expect to ultimately need a formalism that better handles constraints and abstraction (Beckers and Halpern 2019; Blom et al. 2019; Richardson et al. 2025; Geiger et al. 2025), but this is presently out of scope. A causal model WW, intuitively, assigns to each variable vv an equation v=fv​(…)v=f_{v}(\ldots) that determines the value of vv as a function of other variables and independent randomness. Since there are so many variables in our setting, WW cannot be an explicit list of such equations. Therefore, think of WW as a computer program that takes vv as input and produces the function fvf_{v}.
items.zh: 我们的框架还需要对因果性进行形式化。为简单起见，我们基于有限结构因果模型（SCM）（Pearl 2009）33 3 鉴于我们模型的表达力，我们预计最终需要一种能更好处理约束和抽象的形式体系（Beckers and Halpern 2019; Blom et al. 2019; Richardson et al. 2025; Geiger et al. 2025），但这目前超出范围。一个因果模型 WW 直观上为每个变量 vv 赋予一个方程 v=fv​(…)v=f_{v}(\ldots)，该方程将 vv 的值确定为其他变量和独立随机性的函数。由于我们的设置中变量众多，WW 不能是这些方程的显式列表。因此，将 WW 视为一个以 vv 为输入并产生函数 fvf_{v} 的计算机程序。

items.en: A causal model WW (over 𝒱\cal V) associates to each v∈𝒱v\in\cal V a finite set pav⊂𝒱{\rm pa}_{v}\subset\mathcal{V} of causal parents for vv, a finite set of noise variables ηv\eta_{v} encoding independent randomness, and a causal mechanism fv:{0,1}|pav|+|ηv|→{0,1}f_{v}:\{0,1\}^{|{\rm pa}_{v}|+|\eta_{v}|}\to\{0,1\}, which is a function that encodes the equation v=fv​(pav,ηv)v=f_{v}({\rm pa}_{v},\eta_{v}). We identify the causal model WW with a new binary variable, where W=1W=1 implies that all equations in WW (regarded as events that constrain the joint values of 𝒱\cal V) must hold. We consider only causal models with the property that Q~n​(y∣x)\widetilde{Q}_{n}(y\mid x) cannot be causal ancestors of vXiv_{X_{i}} for i⩽ni\leqslant n. Let 𝒲\cal W be the set of all such causal model variables, and let 𝒩\cal N denote the set of all noise variables ηv\eta_{v}, both disjoint from 𝒱\cal V.
items.zh: 一个因果模型 WW（在 𝒱\cal V 上）为每个 v∈𝒱v\in\cal V 关联一个有限的父集 pav⊂𝒱{\rm pa}_{v}\subset\mathcal{V}、一个编码独立随机性的有限噪声变量集 ηv\eta_{v}，以及一个因果机制 fv:{0,1}|pav|+|ηv|→{0,1}f_{v}:\{0,1\}^{|{\rm pa}_{v}|+|\eta_{v}|}\to\{0,1\}，该函数编码了方程 v=fv​(pav,ηv)v=f_{v}({\rm pa}_{v},\eta_{v})。我们将因果模型 WW 视为一个新的二元变量，其中 W=1W=1 意味着 WW 中的所有方程（视为约束 𝒱\cal V 联合值的事件）必须成立。我们只考虑具有如下性质的因果模型：Q~n​(y∣x)\widetilde{Q}_{n}(y\mid x) 不能是 i⩽ni\leqslant n 时 vXiv_{X_{i}} 的因果祖先。令 𝒲\cal W 为所有此类因果模型变量的集合，令 𝒩\cal N 表示所有噪声变量 ηv\eta_{v} 的集合，两者均与 𝒱\cal V 不相交。

items.en: In practice, we further restrict 𝒲\cal W to be consistent with any temporal ordering of variables; for example, the parents of a variable cannot have a later contextualized timestep than the child. We make two further notes.
items.zh: 在实践中，我们进一步限制 𝒲\cal W 与变量的任何时间顺序一致；例如，一个变量的父变量不能具有比子变量更晚的语境化时间步。我们再做两点说明。

items.en: We have identified each W∈𝒲W\in\mathcal{W} by a syntactic description, but also view it as as encoding structural-equation programs over the variables 𝒱\cal V. Each WW is therefore simultaneously a program (specifying mechanisms), a generalized statement (asserting that the equations generated by the program WW hold), and a new Boolean variable taking value 1 only when the assertion holds.
items.zh: 我们已经通过语法描述识别了每个 W∈𝒲W\in\mathcal{W}，但也将其视为对变量 𝒱\cal V 上的结构方程程序的编码。因此，每个 WW 同时是一个程序（指定机制）、一个广义陈述（断言由程序 WW 生成的方程成立）以及一个新的布尔变量，仅当该断言成立时取值为 1。

items.en: The same causal model can be implemented by many different programs; multiple distinct WWs can simultaneously take the value 11, such as those that are syntactically different but semantically equivalent. Program equivalence can be subtle, though, and hence must be learned (to be formalized in Def. 4.5). Furthermore, since we are not handling logical constraints natively, there will be multiple distinct SCMs in our sense that cannot be distinguished (e.g., masskg →\to masslbs vs. masslbs →\to masskg).
items.zh: 同一个因果模型可以由许多不同的程序实现；多个不同的 WW 可以同时取值为 11，例如那些语法不同但语义等价的 WW。程序等价性可能很微妙，因此必须通过学习获得（将在定义 4.5 中形式化）。此外，由于我们并非原生处理逻辑约束，因此在我们意义上存在多个无法区分的不同 SCM（例如，masskg →\to masslbs 与 masslbs →\to masskg）。

items.en: Define 𝒱+:=𝒱∪𝒳∪𝒩∪𝒲\mathcal{V}^{+}:=\mathcal{V}\cup\mathcal{X}\cup\mathcal{N}\cup\mathcal{W} to be the set of all relevant variables to be modeled. The sample space Ω\Omega consists of all joint assignments to 𝒱+\mathcal{V}^{+} with the property that, whenever W=1W=1 for some W∈𝒲W\in\mathcal{W}, then all equations of the causal model WW (interpreted as constraints on 𝒱\mathcal{V}) must hold. For a joint setting ω∈Ω\omega\in\Omega of all variables and a language element x∈ℒx\in\mathcal{L}, write ω⊧x\omega\models x if xx is true in world ω\omega. Let P∗P^{*} be the unknown “true” probability measure over Ω\Omega. We further assume that the marginal P∗​(𝒩)P^{*}(\mathcal{N}) on noise variables is uniform.
items.zh: 定义 𝒱+:=𝒱∪𝒳∪𝒩∪𝒲\mathcal{V}^{+}:=\mathcal{V}\cup\mathcal{X}\cup\mathcal{N}\cup\mathcal{W} 为所有待建模的相关变量的集合。样本空间 Ω\Omega 由 𝒱+\mathcal{V}^{+} 的所有联合赋值组成，且满足：只要对于某个 W∈𝒲W\in\mathcal{W} 有 W=1W=1，则因果模型 WW 的所有方程（解释为对 𝒱\mathcal{V} 的约束）必须成立。对于所有变量的一个联合设定 ω∈Ω\omega\in\Omega 和一个语言元素 x∈ℒx\in\mathcal{L}，如果 xx 在世界 ω\omega 中为真，则记 ω⊧x\omega\models x。令 P∗P^{*} 为 Ω\Omega 上未知的“真实”概率测度。我们进一步假设噪声变量上的边际分布 P∗​(𝒩)P^{*}(\mathcal{N}) 是均匀的。

items.en: Thus P∗P^{*} governs both which variables are collected in the dataset (i.e., the values of 𝒳\mathcal{X}) and what values of these variables are observed (i.e., the corresponding values vXv_{X} which in this case are equal to one). It is possible to extend the algebra of queriable events ℒ\mathcal{L} to ℒ+\mathcal{L}^{+} over all of 𝒱+\mathcal{V}^{+}, by pushing all these concepts into the inductive loop of Def. 3.2.
items.zh: 因此 P∗P^{*} 既控制哪些变量被收集到数据集中（即 𝒳\mathcal{X} 的值），也控制这些变量的观测值（即相应的 vXv_{X} 值，在此情况下等于 1）。可以通过将所有概念推入定义 3.2 的归纳循环中，将可查询事件代数 ℒ\mathcal{L} 扩展到 ℒ+\mathcal{L}^{+} 上，覆盖所有 𝒱+\mathcal{V}^{+}。

items.en: We are trying to model a Bayesian posterior over our (enormous) sample space Ω\Omega. This amounts to selecting a prior (P0P_{0}, defined next) and conditioning on our observations (the dataset DnD_{n}).
items.zh: 我们试图对（庞大的）样本空间 Ω\Omega 上的贝叶斯后验进行建模。这相当于选择一个先验（P0P_{0}，接下来定义）并以我们的观测（数据集 DnD_{n}）为条件。

items.en: Denote by P0P_{0} a prior over Ω\Omega. We require that P0​(W=1)>0P_{0}(W=1)>0 for every W∈𝒲W\in\mathcal{W}, and that 𝒩\mathcal{N} be mutually independent in P0P_{0}. Define the posterior PnP_{n} over Ω\Omega as Pn(⋅)=P0(⋅∣Dn)P_{n}(\cdot)=P_{0}(\cdot\mid D_{n}).
items.zh: 记 P0P_{0} 为 Ω\Omega 上的一个先验。我们要求对于每个 W∈𝒲W\in\mathcal{W} 有 P0​(W=1)>0P_{0}(W=1)>0，并且 𝒩\mathcal{N} 在 P0P_{0} 中相互独立。定义 Ω\Omega 上的后验 PnP_{n} 为 Pn(⋅)=P0(⋅∣Dn)P_{n}(\cdot)=P_{0}(\cdot\mid D_{n})。

items.en: The prior P0P_{0} is intended to be broad and epistemically neutral—for example, a description-length-biased prior over causal models. We posit that Bayesian updating from P0P_{0} to Pn=P0(⋅∣Dn)P_{n}=P_{0}(\cdot\mid D_{n}), when unique, is then an example of a consequence-blind evidence-reweighting step: hypotheses are reweighted by how well they explain the fixed dataset DnD_{n}, and should not depend on whether or not deploying the resulting Predictor would cause some desired or harmful outcome. We posit that under a broad prior over causal models, the mechanisms capable of supporting a difficult harmful trajectory should occupy a small part of model space, as most mechanisms do not encode the coordinated structure needed for such a trajectory. This intuition motivates the sparsity assumptions used later, and its connection to our eventual safety argument is discussed in Appendix C.3; it is supported by Prop. 5.3, which shows the consequence-invariance of PnP_{n}.
