items.zh: 先验 P0P_{0} 旨在广泛且认识论上中立——例如，一个基于描述长度偏好的因果模型先验。我们假定，从 P0P_{0} 到 Pn=P0(⋅∣Dn)P_{n}=P_{0}(\cdot\mid D_{n}) 的贝叶斯更新，当唯一时，是后果盲目的证据重新加权步骤的一个实例：假设根据它们对固定数据集 DnD_{n} 的解释程度重新加权，并且不应依赖于部署所得预测器是否会导致期望或有害的结果。我们假定，在因果模型的广泛先验下，能够支持困难有害轨迹的机制应占据模型空间的一小部分，因为大多数机制并不编码此类轨迹所需的协调结构。这一直觉激发了后面使用的稀疏性假设，其与我们最终安全性论证的联系在附录 C.3 中讨论；命题 5.3 支持了这一点，该命题显示了 PnP_{n} 的后果不变性。

items.en: A causal model WW is correct if conditioning the prior on it yields P∗P^{*}—that is, if P0(⋅∣W)=P∗(⋅)P_{0}(\cdot\mid W)=P^{*}(\,\cdot\,). Let 𝒲∗⊆𝒲\mathcal{W}^{*}\subseteq\mathcal{W} denote the set of correct causal models.
items.zh: 一个因果模型 WW 是正确的，如果以它为条件的先验等于 P∗P^{*}——即，如果 P0(⋅∣W)=P∗(⋅)P_{0}(\cdot\mid W)=P^{*}(\,\cdot\,)。令 𝒲∗⊆𝒲\mathcal{W}^{*}\subseteq\mathcal{W} 表示正确因果模型的集合。

items.en: The class 𝒲∗\mathcal{W}^{*} of correct models is non-empty.
items.zh: 正确模型类 𝒲∗\mathcal{W}^{*} 非空。

items.en: Req. 3.11 is supported by the generality of the language (natural language, mathematics, and computer code of unrestricted length) used to express causal models, though with a prior that exponentially prefers shorter description length models). Relying on the Church–Turing thesis (Turing 1936), every known scientific model would be expressible in this way.44 4 See Bengio et al. 2025b for a longer discussion of how multiple scientific theories with overlapping claims (such as physics and chemistry) can coexist in the same causal model WW. The combination of insufficient data and computational limitations means that there will be multiple theories that remain competitive (like physics and chemistry), which motivates the SAI objective of approximating the Bayesian posterior under bounded compute.
items.zh: 要求 3.11 得到用于表达因果模型的语言（自然语言、数学和无限长度的计算机代码）的通用性的支持，尽管先验指数级地偏好更短描述长度的模型。依赖丘奇-图灵论题（Turing 1936），每个已知的科学模型都可以用这种方式表达。44 4 参见 Bengio et al. 2025b 关于多个具有重叠主张的科学理论（如物理学和化学）如何共存于同一个因果模型 WW 中的更详细讨论。数据不足和计算限制的结合意味着存在多个仍然有竞争力的理论（如物理学和化学），这激发了在有限算力下近似贝叶斯后验的 SAI 目标。

items.en: The set of variables that can, in principle, have a posterior probability of zero or one will be of particular importance for formalizing our central assumption about “neutrality” of the data-collection process.
items.zh: 原则上后验概率可能为零或一的变量集合，对于形式化我们关于数据收集过程“中立性”的核心假设将特别重要。

items.en: A variable v∈𝒱+v\in\mathcal{V}^{+} is deducible from an event EE (e.g., a value assignment to other variables) if P0​(E)>0P_{0}(E)>0 and P0​(v=1∣E)∈{0,1}.P_{0}(v{=}1\mid E)\in\{0,1\}.
items.zh: 一个变量 v∈𝒱+v\in\mathcal{V}^{+} 可从事件 EE（例如，其他变量的一个值赋值）中推导出，如果 P0​(E)>0P_{0}(E)>0 且 P0​(v=1∣E)∈{0,1}.P_{0}(v{=}1\mid E)\in\{0,1\}.

items.en: Deducibility can be computationally expensive to establish; accordingly, QQ is not assumed to identify deducible variables, and inherits PnP_{n}’s commitments on them only as far as approximation and compute allow. We will later see that this is the gap controlled by epistemic caution (Prop. 4.14).
items.zh: 推导性在计算上可能代价高昂；因此，QQ 不假定能识别可推导变量，并且仅在近似和算力允许的范围内继承 PnP_{n} 对它们的承诺。我们稍后将看到，这是由认识论谨慎性控制的间隙（命题 4.14）。

items.en: Let 𝒪⊂𝒱+\mathcal{O}\subset\mathcal{V}^{+} be the set of potentially observable variables. We require that 𝒪\mathcal{O} contain all statement-variables in 𝒱\mathcal{V} that may be directly observed, all selection variables Xi∈𝒳X_{i}\in\mathcal{X} that specify which statement-variable was observed, and all variables whose values are, in principle, deducible from the values of directly observed variables. We call variables in 𝒱+∖𝒪\mathcal{V}^{+}\setminus\mathcal{O} unobservable. We write D∞D_{\infty} for the limiting directly observed dataset and P∞P_{\infty} for P0​(y∣x,D∞)P_{0}(y\mid x,D_{\infty}). Both D∞D_{\infty} and P∞P_{\infty} are random variables over Ω\Omega whose distribution is governed by P∗P^{*}.
items.zh: 设 𝒪⊂𝒱⁺ 为潜在可观测变量的集合。我们要求 𝒪 包含所有可能被直接观测的 𝒱 中的陈述变量、指定观测了哪个陈述变量的选择变量 Xi∈𝒳，以及原则上可从直接观测变量的值推导出的所有变量。我们称 𝒱⁺∖𝒪 中的变量为不可观测变量。记 D∞ 为极限直接观测数据集，P∞ 为 P₀(y∣x,D∞)。D∞ 和 P∞ 都是 Ω 上的随机变量，其分布由 P* 控制。

items.en: The “directly observed” clause of Def. 3.14 formalizes as P∗(∃n.Xn=s)>0P^{*}(\exists n.~X_{n}=s)>0. Note that D∞D_{\infty} will not include all of 𝒪\mathcal{O}, because some variables (e.g., those with a specific time stamp) might never be observed despite being observable in principle. D∞D_{\infty} will also not contain all deducible consequences because of limited compute. Unobservable statements include things like the intentions, thoughts, or beliefs of agents; the best we can generally do about them is infer their probability. Within 𝒪\mathcal{O}, we are particularly interested in epistemic/probabilistic consequences, such as statements of the form Pn​(y∣x)<bP_{n}(y\mid x)<b, since they allow us to form confidence intervals over queries.
items.zh: 定义 3.14 的“直接观测”条款形式化为 P*(∃n. Xₙ=s)>0。注意 D∞ 不会包含 𝒪 中的所有变量，因为某些变量（例如具有特定时间戳的变量）尽管原则上可观测，但可能从未被观测到。由于算力有限，D∞ 也不会包含所有可推导的结论。不可观测陈述包括智能体的意图、思想或信念等；我们通常能对它们做的最好的事情就是推断其概率。在 𝒪 中，我们特别关注认知/概率性后果，例如形如 Pₙ(y∣x)<b 的陈述，因为它们允许我们为查询构建置信区间。

items.en: With these definitions in place, we can now state the key technical requirement we need in order to ensure that the data-collection process reveals enough about the world in the limit: intuitively the dataset DnD_{n} must provide more and more information about the true causal structure in P∗P^{*} as nn increases.
items.zh: 有了这些定义，我们现在可以陈述确保数据收集过程在极限情况下充分揭示世界的关键技术要求：直观上，随着 n 增加，数据集 Dₙ 必须提供越来越多关于 P* 中真实因果结构的信息。

items.en: The dataset is informative about P∗P^{*}. Formally, the mutual information IP0​(Dn,𝒲)I_{P_{0}}(D_{n};\mathcal{W}) under the prior P0P_{0} (of Def. 3.8) converges to IP0​(𝒪,𝒲)I_{P_{0}}(\mathcal{O};\mathcal{W}) as n→∞n\to\infty.
items.zh: 数据集关于 P* 是信息性的。形式上，在先验 P₀（定义 3.8）下，互信息 I_{P₀}(Dₙ;𝒲) 当 n→∞ 时收敛于 I_{P₀}(𝒪;𝒲)。

items.en: Beyond informativeness, it is important that the data not be adversarially constructed to cause a particular downstream effect with the deployment of the Bayesian posterior. We believe it would be difficult to select data this way, particularly in the presence of the contextualization (Def. 3.22), and very unlikely to happen by accident. Excluding such data-selection attacks lies outside the scope of the present analysis.
items.zh: 除了信息性之外，数据不应被对抗性地构造以在贝叶斯后验部署时引起特定的下游效应。我们认为，特别是在情境化（定义 3.22）存在的情况下，很难通过这种方式选择数据，并且偶然发生的可能性极低。排除此类数据选择攻击不在当前分析范围内。

full.sec: 3.2 The Predictor, and Requirements Imposed During Training

items.en: The predictor QQ, whose definition and training are the focus of this section, is trained towards the Bayesian posterior on a fixed dataset DnD_{n} (and not to resolve uncertainty any further than that). From this point forward, we therefore fix DnD_{n}, and hence the integer nn, the values of the selection variables X1,…,XnX_{1},\ldots,X_{n}, and their realized values x1,…,xnx_{1},\ldots,x_{n}. We also drop the subscript on Q~:=Q~n\widetilde{Q}:=\widetilde{Q}_{n}, which we now begin to interpret.
items.zh: 预测器 Q 的定义和训练是本节的焦点，它在固定数据集 Dₙ 上朝着贝叶斯后验训练（并且不进一步解决不确定性）。从现在起，我们固定 Dₙ，因此固定整数 n、选择变量 X₁,…,Xₙ 的值及其实现值 x₁,…,xₙ。我们还去掉 Q̃:=Q̃ₙ 的下标，现在开始解释它。

items.en: Let 𝒱past⊂𝒱+\mathcal{V}_{\rm past}\subset\mathcal{V}^{+} be a subset of variables containing 𝒲\mathcal{W}, the statements in DnD_{n}, X1,…,XnX_{1},\ldots,X_{n}, and every variable that can be a causal ancestor of some statement in DnD_{n} under some causal model W∈𝒲W\in\mathcal{W}—but not Q~\widetilde{Q}. Define the future variables by 𝒱future:=𝒱+∖𝒱past.{\cal V}_{\rm future}:={\cal V}^{+}\setminus{\cal V}_{\rm past}. The variables of Q~​(y∣x)\widetilde{Q}(y\mid x) must therefore be in the future.
items.zh: 设 𝒱_past⊂𝒱⁺ 为包含 𝒲、Dₙ 中的陈述、X₁,…,Xₙ 以及每个在某个因果模型 W∈𝒲 下可以是 Dₙ 中某个陈述的因果祖先的变量——但不包含 Q̃ 的变量子集。通过 𝒱_future:=𝒱⁺∖𝒱_past 定义未来变量。因此 Q̃(y∣x) 的变量必须在未来中。

items.en: Some variables can be constant (not have variants that differ across time), in which case they should be in 𝒱√⊣∫⊔\cal V_{\rm past} if they can cause elements of DnD_{n}.
items.zh: 某些变量可以是常数（没有随时间变化的变体），在这种情况下，如果它们能引起 Dₙ 的元素，则它们应属于 𝒱_past。

items.en: Let 𝚀⊆ℒ×ℒ\mathtt{Q}\subseteq\mathcal{L}\times\mathcal{L} be a fixed countable family of queries of bounded token length. Let 𝒬:=(0,1)𝚀\mathcal{Q}:=(0,1)^{\mathtt{Q}} and 𝒬¯:=[0,1]𝚀\overline{\mathcal{Q}}:=[0,1]^{\mathtt{Q}}. A (probabilistic) Predictor Q∈𝒬Q\in\mathcal{Q} is a (stateless55 5 Statelessness means that QQ’s output depends only on the current query (x,y)(x,y) and not on any internal record of queries previously evaluated during deployment; in particular, QQ maintains no writable memory that persists across query evaluations.) function that assigns to each (x,y)∈𝚀(x,y)\in\mathtt{Q} a number Q⁡(y∣x)∈(0,1)Q(y\mid x)\in(0,1), represented in binary to precision BB. The Bayesian posterior PnP_{n} itself, viewed as the mapping (x,y)↦Pn​(y∣x)(x,y)\mapsto P_{n}(y\mid x), is an element of 𝒬¯\overline{\mathcal{Q}}.
items.zh: 设 𝚀⊆ℒ×ℒ 为有界令牌长度的固定可数查询族。设 𝒬:=(0,1)^𝚀 和 𝒬̅:=[0,1]^𝚀。一个（概率性）预测器 Q∈𝒬 是一个（无状态⁵⁵ 无状态意味着 Q 的输出仅依赖于当前查询 (x,y)，而不依赖于部署期间先前评估的查询的任何内部记录；特别是，Q 不维护跨查询评估持续存在的可写内存。）函数，它将每个 (x,y)∈𝚀 映射到一个数 Q(y∣x)∈(0,1)，以二进制表示，精度为 B。贝叶斯后验 Pₙ 本身，视为映射 (x,y)↦Pₙ(y∣x)，是 𝒬̅ 的一个元素。

items.en: In practice, 𝚀\mathtt{Q} will consist of (x,y)(x,y) pairs with yy short, and xx in conjunctive normal form. The posterior target Pn​(y∣x)P_{n}(y\mid x) may be 00 or 11 for deterministic observations, code outputs, proven theorems, contradictions, or other deductive consequences of the contextualized data. For training purposes, it is often convenient to restrict finite Predictor outputs to (0,1)(0,1), working with logits, so that losses and gradients remain well-behaved. This is a training convention only: exact posterior targets are represented in the closure 𝒬¯=[0,1]𝚀\overline{\mathcal{Q}}=[0,1]^{\mathtt{Q}}, and trainable Predictors may approach them as limits.
items.zh: 在实践中，𝚀 将由 (x,y) 对组成，其中 y 较短，x 为合取范式。后验目标 Pₙ(y∣x) 对于确定性观测、代码输出、已证明定理、矛盾或情境化数据的其他演绎结果可能为 0 或 1。出于训练目的，通常将有限预测器输出限制在 (0,1)，使用 logits，以便损失和梯度保持良好行为。这仅是训练约定：精确后验目标在闭包 𝒬̅=[0,1]^𝚀 中表示，可训练预测器可以作为极限接近它们。

items.en: We write Q⁡(y∣x)Q(y\mid x) for the scalar prediction at (x,y)(x,y), and use the symbol QQ alone in expressions like J⁡(Q)J(Q), for the Predictor as a function, allowing context to disambiguate. In particular, throughout the paper, we use the symbol PnP_{n} for both the joint distribution Pn∈Δ​ΩP_{n}\in\Delta\Omega and its evaluation on the bounded query family, Pn:𝚀→[0,1]P_{n}:\mathtt{Q}\to[0,1], that assigns (x,y)∈𝚀(x,y)\in\mathtt{Q} to Pn​(y=1∣x=1)P_{n}(y=1\mid x=1).
items.zh: 我们写 Q(y∣x) 表示在 (x,y) 处的标量预测，并在诸如 J(Q) 的表达式中单独使用符号 Q 表示作为函数的预测器，允许上下文消除歧义。特别地，在整篇论文中，我们使用符号 Pₙ 表示联合分布 Pₙ∈ΔΩ 及其在有界查询族上的评估 Pₙ:𝚀→[0,1]，该评估将 (x,y)∈𝚀 映射到 Pₙ(y=1∣x=1)。

items.en: The rest of this subsection imposes requirements on the process of training QQ, which we now define.
items.zh: 本小节的其余部分对训练 Q 的过程施加要求，我们现在定义该过程。

items.en: The training process is a distribution ν\nu over training trajectories indexed by tt, with randomness due, for example, to initialization, minibatch sampling, and SGD noise. We write νt\nu_{t} for its marginal over Predictors at step tt.
items.zh: 训练过程是一个关于由 t 索引的训练轨迹的分布 ν，其随机性例如来自初始化、小批量采样和 SGD 噪声。我们写 νₜ 表示其在步骤 t 时预测器上的边缘分布。

items.en: The process of transforming syntactic objects into semantically valid strings (i.e., statements) is part of the process of epistemic contextualization. The Predictor is then trained on this epistemically contextualized dataset; it takes as input queries, and outputs probability distributions for valid queries. We now introduce a series of assumptions and requirements for training the SAI Predictor.
items.zh: 将语法对象转换为语义有效字符串（即陈述）的过程是认知情境化过程的一部分。然后，预测器在这个认知情境化的数据集上训练；它接收查询作为输入，并为有效查询输出概率分布。我们现在介绍训练 SAI 预测器的一系列假设和要求。

items.en: Let a record be an observation in the sense of Def. 3.4. A dataset is epistemically contextualized (or just contextualized) when its syntax marks it as exactly one of two kinds. A factual observation records a directly measured value, the output of an executed computation, or a proven statement. In contrast, a communication act records that a named agent uttered something, possibly identified as a stated claim about a property of the world, and it carries metadata identifying the source, venue, and timestamp. A dataset is contextualized when every record in it is contextualized; we call the process of transforming data sources into such records epistemic contextualization.
items.zh: 设记录为定义 3.4 意义上的观测。当数据集的语法将其标记为恰好两种类型之一时，该数据集是认知情境化的（或简称为情境化的）。事实观测记录直接测量的值、已执行计算的输出或已证明的陈述。相比之下，通信行为记录一个命名智能体说了某事，可能被识别为关于世界属性的声称，并携带标识来源、地点和时间戳的元数据。当数据集中的每条记录都是情境化的时，该数据集是情境化的；我们将数据源转换为此类记录的过程称为认知情境化。

items.en: Communication-act records assert that the act occurred (with the stated metadata), not that the communicated content is true. This syntactic separation is used to prevent uncontextualized statements from being treated as ground-truth facts. Contextualization defaults to communication acts unless the record is a direct measurement or a proven statement. We illustrate this with an example and then impose a necessary requirement of our pipeline.
items.zh: 通信行为记录断言该行为发生了（带有声明的元数据），而不是所传达的内容为真。这种语法分离用于防止未情境化的陈述被当作事实真相。除非记录是直接测量或已证明的陈述，否则情境化默认为通信行为。我们用一个例子说明这一点，然后对我们的流程施加一个必要的要求。

items.en: Verified facts use factual syntax: e.g., “The Earth orbits the Sun,” or the confirmed output of a computation. By contrast, a source claim is recorded as a communication act: “The Flat Earth Society FAQ stated in 2025: The Earth is constantly accelerating upward at 9.8​m/s29.8\,\mathrm{m/s}^{2}’’.66 6 https://wiki.tfes.org/Flat_Earth_-_Frequently_Asked_Questions The record asserts only that the claim was made; the underlying Flat Earth assertion is treated as a latent hypothesis, plausibly assigned negligible posterior probability because it is incoherent with the bulk of the data. Author metadata (venue, timestamp, identity) are included in communication-act records, enabling the SAI to weight sources by their reliability.
items.zh: 验证的事实使用事实语法：例如，“地球绕太阳公转”，或计算的确切输出。相比之下，来源声明被记录为通信行为：“扁平地球学会 FAQ 在 2025 年声明：地球以 9.8 m/s² 持续向上加速”。⁶⁶ https://wiki.tfes.org/Flat_Earth_-_Frequently_Asked_Questions 该记录仅断言该声明被做出；潜在的扁平地球断言被视为一个潜在假设，由于与大部分数据不一致，其被赋予可忽略的后验概率。作者元数据（地点、时间戳、身份）包含在通信行为记录中，使 SAI 能够根据来源的可靠性为其加权。

items.en: The SAI Predictor QQ is only trained on contextualized data.
items.zh: SAI 预测器 Q 仅在情境化数据上训练。

items.en: A factual observation is a directly observed statement-variable, and a communication act is the directly observed event that an agent made an utterance, so both kinds of record name directly observed statement-variables, and thus get placed in 𝒪\mathcal{O} (see Def. 3.22 and Def. 3.14), with the selection variables Xi∈𝒳X_{i}\in\mathcal{X} lying in 𝒪\mathcal{O} for the same reason. Hence, every contextualized record is observable, and the membership xi∈𝒪x_{i}\in\mathcal{O} holds by Def. 3.4 and Def. 3.22.
items.zh: 事实观测是直接观测的陈述变量，通信行为是智能体做出话语的直接观测事件，因此两种记录都命名直接观测的陈述变量，从而被放入 𝒪（见定义 3.22 和定义 3.14），选择变量 Xi∈𝒳 同样位于 𝒪 中。因此，每条情境化记录都是可观测的，并且根据定义 3.4 和定义 3.22，成员关系 xi∈𝒪 成立。

items.en: Epistemic contextualization may introduce rare errors (e.g., mis-specified observations). In safety-critical use, such uncertainty should be handled conservatively by increasing abstention/uncertainty, and by using data-quality checks to flag suspicious observations. Our safety guarantees rely on the SAI Predictor being trained on a contextualized dataset of sufficient size to achieve the required semantic generalization (see our later Assumption 4.11). When the semantic anchoring of a factual latent is weak, the appropriate behavior is similarly calibrated uncertainty or abstention. Hence, the SAI Predictor is not initialized from a pre-trained LLM. However, experiments in which an SAI Predictor is initialized from a pre-trained LLM and then fine-tuned with the SAI objective and contextualized data could serve as evidence of the comparative safety and reasoning accuracy advantages of the SAI framework.
items.zh: 认知情境化可能引入罕见错误（例如，错误指定的观测）。在安全关键应用中，这种不确定性应通过增加弃权/不确定性以及使用数据质量检查来标记可疑观测来保守处理。我们的安全保证依赖于 SAI 预测器在足够大的情境化数据集上训练，以达到所需的语义泛化（见我们后面的假设 4.11）。当事实潜在变量的语义锚定较弱时，适当的行为是类似校准的不确定性或弃权。因此，SAI 预测器不是从预训练 LLM 初始化的。然而，从预训练 LLM 初始化 SAI 预测器，然后使用 SAI 目标和情境化数据进行微调的实验，可以作为 SAI 框架在安全性和推理准确性方面优势的证据。

items.en: To motivate the next requirement, consider that conditioning on DnD_{n} updates beliefs (the posterior PnP_{n} and its estimator QQ) but does not change the world. Deploying those predictions, however, as modeled by variable Q~​(y∣x)\widetilde{Q}(y\mid x) for a query (x,y)(x,y) can affect future events, as it is an action that is best modeled causally. Recall that we regard the variable(s) Q~n​(y∣x)\widetilde{Q}_{n}(y\mid x) as the (binary representation of the) deployed predictor’s output probability. Thus, the intervention do​(Q~​(y∣x)=q)\mathrm{do}(\widetilde{Q}(y\mid x)=q) sets the predictor’s output to qq in our model.
items.zh: 为了激励下一个要求，考虑以 D_n 为条件会更新信念（后验 P_n 及其估计量 Q），但不会改变世界。然而，部署这些预测（如变量 Q~(y|x) 对查询 (x,y) 所建模的）可能会影响未来事件，因为它是一种最好用因果方式建模的行动。回想一下，我们将变量 Q~_n(y|x) 视为已部署预测器输出概率的（二进制表示）。因此，干预 do(Q~(y|x)=q) 在我们的模型中将预测器的输出设置为 q。

items.en: The predictor must be trained to correctly answer interventional queries of the form Q⁡(y∣x,do⁡(Q~​(a∣b)=q))Q(y\mid x,~{\rm do}(\widetilde{Q}(a\mid b)=q)). Furthermore, when deployed predictions Q~\widetilde{Q} are variables in roll-outs involving downstream effects and used to update QQ itself (updating parameters at training time or predictions themselves at run-time) no signal flows back through Q~\widetilde{Q} into QQ.
items.zh: 预测器必须经过训练，以正确回答形式为 Q(y|x, do(Q~(a|b)=q)) 的干预性查询。此外，当已部署的预测 Q~ 是涉及下游影响的展开中的变量，并用于更新 Q 本身（在训练时更新参数或在运行时更新预测本身）时，没有信号通过 Q~ 流回 Q。

items.en: Req. 3.28 can be implemented by exposing the Predictor during training to interventional statements written with do(⋅){\rm do}(\cdot)-syntax, generated for example from sampled causal subgraphs. Furthermore, we can isolate the effect of Q~\tilde{Q} by intentionally creating toy opportunities for performative prediction, and including the results in the dataset. The key requirement is semantic: QQ must interpret deployed predictions as interventions: adding do​(Q~​(b∣a)=q){\rm do}(\tilde{Q}(b\mid a)=q) as context to a query only alters QQ’s probabilistic assessment of 𝒱future\mathcal{V}_{\rm future}, and not 𝒱past\mathcal{V}_{\rm past}.
items.zh: 要求 3.28 可以通过在训练期间让预测器暴露于用 do(·) 语法编写的干预性语句来实现，例如从采样的因果子图生成。此外，我们可以通过有意创建表演性预测的玩具机会并将结果包含在数据集中来隔离 Q~ 的影响。关键要求是语义上的：Q 必须将已部署的预测解释为干预：将 do(Q~(b|a)=q) 作为上下文添加到查询中只会改变 Q 对 V_future 的概率评估，而不会改变 V_past。

items.en: Technically speaking, do​(Q~​(a∣b)=q)∉ℒ\mathrm{do}(\tilde{Q}(a\mid b)=q)\notin\mathcal{L}, and hence these interventional queries are not part of our model as defined. However, intervention can be viewed as conditioning on a response variable (Rubin 1974; Balke and Pearl 1994) even in generalized causal models (Richardson et al. 2025). To make this type-check, we would need to take causal mechanisms, which are currently determined by WW, as primitive, and then absorb the definition of causal models (among other things) within the mutual induction of Def. 3.2. We leave this level of rigor for a future endeavor where we can benefit from it.
items.zh: 从技术上讲，do(Q~(a|b)=q) ∉ L，因此这些干预性查询不属于我们定义的模型。然而，干预可以被视为以响应变量为条件（Rubin 1974; Balke and Pearl 1994），即使在广义因果模型中也是如此（Richardson et al. 2025）。为了使这种类型检查通过，我们需要将当前由 W 确定的因果机制作为原语，然后在定义 3.2 的相互归纳中吸收因果模型的定义（以及其他内容）。我们将这种严格性水平留给未来的工作，届时我们可以从中受益。

items.en: For the current training run, DnD_{n} denotes the records already observed and held fixed as training data. Post-deployment consequences of the SAI Predictor’s outputs are modeled as future variables and may be queried via interventional predictives; however, they do not feed back into the posterior or training objective of that same run. Thus, the current Bayesian posterior over causal models depends only on the fixed realized dataset DnD_{n}. Given P0P_{0}, DnD_{n}, and a query (x,y)(x,y), the posterior conditional Pn(y∣x)=P0(y=1∣x=1,Dn)P_{n}(y\mid x)=P_{0}(y=1\mid x=1,D_{n}) is uniquely defined whenever P0(x=1,Dn)>0P_{0}(x=1,D_{n})>0. If some deployment consequences are later observed, they may be incorporated only as ordinary contextualized observations in a future enlarged dataset Dn′⊇DnD_{n^{\prime}}\supseteq D_{n} for a subsequent training run. Sampling downstream cause–effect chains within the SAI training procedure serves only to define coherent posteriors. Treating deployed predictions as interventions, therefore, does not introduce a fixed-point ambiguity into the Bayesian target itself, and any performative fixed-point issue arises only when the Scaffold chooses what to deploy (see Sec. 3.3).
items.zh: 对于当前的训练运行，D_n 表示已经观察并固定为训练数据的记录。SAI 预测器输出的部署后后果被建模为未来变量，并可以通过干预性预测进行查询；然而，它们不会反馈到同一运行的后验或训练目标中。因此，当前关于因果模型的贝叶斯后验仅取决于固定的已实现数据集 D_n。给定 P_0、D_n 和查询 (x,y)，后验条件概率 P_n(y|x) = P_0(y=1|x=1, D_n) 在 P_0(x=1, D_n) > 0 时是唯一确定的。如果某些部署后果后来被观察到，它们只能作为普通的情境化观察被纳入未来更大的数据集 D_n' ⊇ D_n 中，用于后续的训练运行。在 SAI 训练过程中对下游因果链进行采样仅用于定义一致的后验。因此，将已部署的预测视为干预不会在贝叶斯目标本身中引入不动点歧义，任何表演性不动点问题仅在 Scaffold 选择部署什么时出现（见第 3.3 节）。

items.en: For each y∈𝒱futurey\in\mathcal{V}_{\rm future}, there exists y′∈𝒱pasty^{\prime}\in\mathcal{V}_{\rm past} such that the causal mechanisms fy=fy′f_{y}=f_{y^{\prime}} are identical. In other words, a single mechanism object is shared between yy and y′y^{\prime}, so future predictions must reuse mechanism uncertainty already constrained by past data.
items.zh: 对于每个 y ∈ V_future，存在 y' ∈ V_past，使得因果机制 f_y = f_y' 相同。换句话说，一个单一的机制对象在 y 和 y' 之间共享，因此未来预测必须重用已经由过去数据约束的机制不确定性。

items.en: Req. 3.32 is not satisfied unless the future variables draw on the identical mechanism objects already present in 𝒱past\mathcal{V}_{\rm past}. This formalizes the assumption that causal laws are time-invariant, where the mechanisms governing future events are those governing analogous past events (e.g., the same laws of physics apply throughout time and space). With more data we may legitimately change our beliefs about which mechanism is correct (i.e., the posterior weight Pn​(W=1)P_{n}(W=1) assigned to each causal model WW), but the time-invariance of causal laws ensures that there will exist an identical causal mechanism fy=fy′f_{y}=f_{y^{\prime}} for each (y′,y)∈𝒱past×𝒱future(y^{\prime},y)\in\mathcal{V}_{\rm past}\times\mathcal{V}_{\rm future}.77 7 See Bengio et al. 2025b for further discussion and Deleu et al. 2022 for an early implementation. Note further that PnP_{n} is an idealized intractable target in general; throughout, “approaches PnP_{n}” should be read as “approaches PnP_{n} up to approximation error determined by the compute budget and modeling restrictions,” with the quantification of this computational uncertainty left to future work.
items.zh: 除非未来变量利用 V_past 中已经存在的相同机制对象，否则要求 3.32 不满足。这形式化了因果定律是时间不变的假设，其中支配未来事件的机制与支配类似过去事件的机制相同（例如，相同的物理定律适用于所有时间和空间）。随着更多数据，我们可以合法地改变我们对哪个机制正确的信念（即每个因果模型 W 的后验权重 P_n(W=1)），但因果定律的时间不变性确保对于每个 (y', y) ∈ V_past × V_future，存在相同的因果机制 f_y = f_y'。

items.en: The key thing that we gain from a causal model of the world, including predictor outputs, is the ability to disconnect the training signal from realized outcomes in the future. We discuss this at length in Sec. 5.1; for now, we simply assert that training towards the Bayesian posterior is one way of achieving this intuitively (modulo performative prediction, which we handle in Sec. 3.3).
items.zh: 我们从包含预测器输出的世界因果模型中获得的关键能力是断开训练信号与未来已实现结果的联系。我们在第 5.1 节中详细讨论这一点；目前，我们只是断言朝向贝叶斯后验训练是实现这一目标的一种直观方式（除了表演性预测，我们在第 3.3 节中处理）。

items.en: The SAI Predictor’s training process ν\nu is generated by optimizing a non-negative objective JJ, defined on 𝒬¯\overline{\mathcal{Q}}, whose unique global minimizer is the Bayesian posterior PnP_{n}:
items.zh: SAI 预测器的训练过程 ν 是通过优化定义在 Q̅ 上的非负目标 J 生成的，其唯一的全局最小化器是贝叶斯后验 P_n：

items.en: Equivalently, J⁡(Q)=0J(Q)=0 if and only if Q⁡(y∣x)=Pn​(y∣x)Q(y\mid x)=P_{n}(y\mid x) for every (x,y)∈𝚀(x,y)\in\mathtt{Q}. For trainable Predictors Q∈𝒬=(0,1)𝚀Q\in\mathcal{Q}=(0,1)^{\mathtt{Q}}, the case where Pn​(y∣x)∈{0,1}P_{n}(y\mid x)\in\{0,1\} is interpreted as a boundary target in 𝒬¯\overline{\mathcal{Q}}. Thus the requirement is that PnP_{n} is the unique minimizer of the extended objective on 𝒬¯\overline{\mathcal{Q}}, and that any minimizing sequence Qk∈𝒬Q_{k}\in\mathcal{Q} converges coordinatewise to PnP_{n}.
items.zh: 等价地，J(Q)=0 当且仅当对于每个 (x,y) ∈ Q，Q(y|x) = P_n(y|x)。对于可训练的预测器 Q ∈ Q = (0,1)^Q，P_n(y|x) ∈ {0,1} 的情况被解释为 Q̅ 中的边界目标。因此，要求是 P_n 是 Q̅ 上扩展目标的唯一最小化器，并且任何最小化序列 Q_k ∈ Q 坐标收敛到 P_n。

items.en: The Bayesian posterior PnP_{n} is used as the honest reference because it has no separate reporting policy or downstream objective. Its answer to a query is its posterior probability, determined by P0P_{0}, DnD_{n}, and the model class. While PnP_{n} may be uncertain or mistaken under misspecification or insufficient data, the relevant point is that it is posterior-faithful and non-strategic. In particular, PnP_{n} is invariant to re-labeling of future outcomes that preserve the prior (Prop. 5.3).
items.zh: 贝叶斯后验 P_n 被用作诚实的参考，因为它没有独立的报告策略或下游目标。它对查询的回答是其后验概率，由 P_0、D_n 和模型类确定。虽然 P_n 在错误指定或数据不足时可能不确定或错误，但相关点是它是后验忠实且非策略性的。特别地，P_n 对保留先验的未来结果的重新标记是不变的（命题 5.3）。

items.en: This alone is not enough to prevent implicit agency in practice. Even with the Bayesian target PnP_{n} formed from P0P_{0} and DnD_{n} by neutral evidence reweighting, we must also ensure that the training process likewise avoids introducing any explicit preference for downstream deployment effects. We begin to do this as follows:
items.zh: 仅凭这一点不足以在实践中防止隐式智能体行为。即使贝叶斯目标 P_n 由 P_0 和 D_n 通过中性证据重新加权形成，我们也必须确保训练过程同样避免引入任何对下游部署效果的显式偏好。我们开始如下操作：

items.en: The objective JJ, update rule, and checkpoint-selection rule used by the training process ν\nu must not contain any algorithmic signal that rewards, penalizes, ranks, or selects a Predictor because of the realized, predicted, or desired downstream consequences of deploying its answers.
items.zh: 训练过程 ν 使用的目标 J、更新规则和检查点选择规则不得包含任何算法信号，该信号因部署其答案的已实现、预测或期望的下游后果而奖励、惩罚、排名或选择预测器。

items.en: But is a lack of explicit signal enough? What about implicit signals? We return to this in Sec. 5.1.
items.zh: 但缺乏显式信号就足够了吗？隐式信号呢？我们在第 5.1 节中回到这一点。

items.en: At last, we can interpret the variables Q~\widetilde{Q} defined at the very beginning of Sec. 3.1.
items.zh: 最后，我们可以解释在第 3.1 节开头定义的变量 Q~。

items.en: The deployed Predictor Q~\widetilde{Q} is a function
items.zh: 已部署的预测器 Q~ 是一个函数

items.en: that represents the predictions released by an SAI System for a given fixed dataset DnD_{n}, and whose components Q~​(y∣x)\widetilde{Q}(y\mid x) are elements of 𝒱future⊂𝒱\mathcal{V}_{\rm future}\subset\mathcal{V}. The special symbol 𝚗𝚘𝚙𝚛𝚎𝚍𝚒𝚌𝚝𝚒𝚘𝚗{\tt noprediction} for a query (x,y)(x,y) indicates that no prediction is deployed for that query. Formally, encode this mask as the zero-th bit of Q~​(y∣x)\widetilde{Q}(y\mid x).
items.zh: 表示 SAI 系统针对给定固定数据集 D_n 发布的预测，其分量 Q~(y|x) 是 V_future ⊂ V 的元素。对于查询 (x,y)，特殊符号 noprediction 表示没有为该查询部署预测。形式上，将此掩码编码为 Q~(y|x) 的第零位。

items.en: QQ itself never outputs 𝚗𝚘𝚙𝚛𝚎𝚍𝚒𝚌𝚝𝚒𝚘𝚗{\tt noprediction}, but we do not intend to simply deploy the predictor (i.e., do⁡(Q~=Q){\rm do}(\widetilde{Q}=Q)), as this could lead to misuse. Instead, we wrap QQ within a guardrail, to get the combined system γQ\gamma_{Q}.
items.zh: Q 本身从不输出 noprediction，但我们不打算简单地部署预测器（即 do(Q~=Q)），因为这可能导致滥用。相反，我们将 Q 包装在护栏内，得到组合系统 γ_Q。

items.en: The trained Predictor QQ (Def. 3.19) maps queries to probability estimates approximating PnP_{n}. The deployed Predictor Q~\widetilde{Q} (Def. 3.37) is a collection of variables describing the Scaffold’s actual release, which may differ from QQ (see Def. 3.41 and §2.4). The guardrailed Predictor γQ\gamma_{Q} is the specific deployment policy analyzed in the safety theorem, and will be defined formally (Def. 5.9) in the safety analysis, after we clarify what exactly it is supposed to guard against.
items.zh: 训练后的预测器 Q（定义 3.19）将查询映射到近似 P_n 的概率估计。已部署的预测器 Q~（定义 3.37）是描述 Scaffold 实际发布的变量集合，可能与 Q 不同（见定义 3.41 和 §2.4）。带护栏的预测器 γ_Q 是安全定理中分析的具体部署策略，将在安全分析中正式定义（定义 5.9），在我们澄清它应该防范什么之后。

items.en: An SAI Scaffold is a procedure that calls an SAI Predictor QQ and other tools to produce deployed outputs. An SAI System is the composition of an SAI Scaffold with the Predictor(s) it calls. An SAI Guardrail is an SAI Predictor used to estimate downstream risks of candidate outputs and to reject outputs whose estimated risk exceeds specified thresholds.
items.zh: SAI Scaffold 是一个调用 SAI 预测器 Q 和其他工具以产生部署输出的过程。SAI 系统是 SAI Scaffold 与其调用的预测器的组合。SAI 护栏是一个用于估计候选输出的下游风险并拒绝估计风险超过指定阈值的输出的 SAI 预测器。

items.en: The SAI Scaffold may query both an untrusted agentic model and an SAI Guardrail; the resulting overall SAI System may thus be agentic even if the Predictor QQ is not.
items.zh: SAI 脚手架可以查询不受信任的智能体模型和 SAI 护栏；因此，即使预测器 QQ 不是智能体式的，整个 SAI 系统也可能是智能体式的。

items.en: For a query (x,y)(x,y) to be deployed with prediction qq, the performative prediction consistency constraint requires
items.zh: 对于要部署预测 qq 的查询（x,y）(x,y)，执行性预测一致性约束要求

items.en: That is, the deployed value qq must remain QQ’s prediction after accounting for the causal effect of deploying it. Several such fixed points may exist, or none at all.
items.zh: 也就是说，部署的值 qq 必须是在考虑部署的因果效应后 QQ 的预测。可能存在多个这样的不动点，也可能一个都没有。

items.en: Def. 3.41 states the coherence condition for a deployed prediction; informally, it requires that the prediction the Scaffold releases be one that the Predictor would still endorse after accounting for the causal effect of deploying it (e.g., a price forecast should be consistent with how the market would move upon observing it). Although other predictive interventions are possible, the predictions satisfying this constraint are the most coherent with the SAI Predictor’s posterior model of the world and are thus more accurate.
items.zh: 定义 3.41 陈述了已部署预测的相干条件；非正式地说，它要求脚手架发布的预测是预测器在考虑部署的因果效应后仍然认可的预测（例如，价格预测应与市场在观察到该预测后的变动一致）。尽管可能存在其他预测性干预，满足此约束的预测与 SAI 预测器的世界后验模型最为一致，因此更准确。

items.en: This section provides arguments for expecting the Predictor to be accurate. We begin by introducing: (a) key accuracy targets that our pipeline aims to achieve, and (b) the Explainer, which may be thought of as the SAI’s ‘System 2’ component (Sec. 4.1). Thereafter, we outline assumptions required to achieve our accuracy targets (Sec. 4.2), and close with arguments for the accuracy of the SAI Predictor (Sec. 4.3).
items.zh: 本节提供了预期预测器准确的论证。我们首先介绍：(a) 我们的流程旨在实现的关键准确性目标，以及(b) 解释器，可视为 SAI 的“系统 2”组件（第 4.1 节）。随后，我们概述实现准确性目标所需的假设（第 4.2 节），并以 SAI 预测器准确性的论证结束（第 4.3 节）。

items.en: A confidence classifier is a probability predictor used with two thresholds: it returns True when the predicted probability is sufficiently close to 11, False when it is sufficiently close to 00, and Unknown otherwise.
items.zh: 置信分类器是一种使用两个阈值的概率预测器：当预测概率足够接近 1 时返回 True，足够接近 0 时返回 False，否则返回 Unknown。

items.en: The epistemic-caution failure set at level ρ∈(0,1)\rho\in(0,1) of a Predictor QQ with respect to the posterior target PnP_{n} is
items.zh: 预测器 QQ 相对于后验目标 PnP_{n}在水平ρ∈(0,1)\rho\in(0,1)上的认知谨慎失败集为

items.tex: \mathcal{F}_{\rho}(Q,P_{n}):=\left\{(x,y)\in\mathtt{Q}:\ (Q(y\mid x)>1-\tfrac{\rho}{2}\ \text{and}\ P_{n}(y\mid x)<1-\rho)~\text{or}~(Q(y\mid x)<\tfrac{\rho}{2}\ \text{and}\ P_{n}(y\mid x)>\rho)\right\}.

items.en: That is, ℱρ​(Q,Pn)\mathcal{F}_{\rho}(Q,P_{n}) collects queries on which QQ asserts confidence (within ρ/2\rho/2 of 00 or 11) that PnP_{n} does not share (its probability being at least ρ/2\rho/2 farther from the same extreme). Given a query distribution λn\lambda_{n} on 𝚀\mathtt{Q} and a training-indexed family of Predictors (Qt)t(Q_{t})_{t}, the family is ρ\rho-epistemically cautious under λn\lambda_{n} if λn​(ℱρ​(Qt,Pn))→0\lambda_{n}(\mathcal{F}_{\rho}(Q_{t},P_{n}))\to 0 as training compute tt increases. It is epistemically cautious if this holds for every ρ∈(0,1)\rho\in(0,1). For a fixed finite tt, one can analogously speak of (ρ,ε)(\rho,\varepsilon)-epistemic caution when λn​(ℱρ​(Qt,Pn))≤ε\lambda_{n}(\mathcal{F}_{\rho}(Q_{t},P_{n}))\leq\varepsilon.
items.zh: 即，ℱρ​(Q,Pn)\mathcal{F}_{\rho}(Q,P_{n})收集了 QQ 断言置信度（在 0 或 1 的ρ/2\rho/2 范围内）而 PnP_{n}不共享（其概率距离同一极端至少远ρ/2\rho/2）的查询。给定查询分布λn\lambda_{n}在𝚀\mathtt{Q}上以及训练索引的预测器族（Qt）t(Q_{t})_{t}，如果随着训练算力 tt 增加，λn​(ℱρ​(Qt,Pn))→0\lambda_{n}(\mathcal{F}_{\rho}(Q_{t},P_{n}))\to 0，则该族在λn\lambda_{n}下是ρ\rho-认知谨慎的。如果对于每个ρ∈(0,1)\rho\in(0,1)都成立，则它是认知谨慎的。对于固定的有限 tt，当λn​(ℱρ​(Qt,Pn))≤ε\lambda_{n}(\mathcal{F}_{\rho}(Q_{t},P_{n}))\leq\varepsilon 时，可以类似地称为（ρ,ε）(\rho,\varepsilon)-认知谨慎。

items.en: The SAI training procedure associates with the conditional probability Predictor QQ a trained generative proposal model, called the Explainer, which samples a small set of candidate latent statement-variables, as well as optional structure over them (e.g., a chain or a directed acyclic graph determining how to factor a joint probability over latents as a product of conditional probabilities), relevant to explain any given query (x,y)(x,y). The Explainer proposes which latent statements to consider; their truth values are sampled from the Predictor QQ itself.
items.zh: SAI 训练过程将条件概率预测器 QQ 与一个训练好的生成式提议模型（称为解释器）相关联，该模型采样一小组候选潜在陈述变量，以及它们之上的可选结构（例如，确定如何将潜在变量上的联合概率分解为条件概率乘积的链或有向无环图），以解释任何给定的查询（x,y）(x,y)。解释器提议要考虑哪些潜在陈述；它们的真值由预测器 QQ 本身采样。

items.en: The Explainer has two roles and is a computational-efficiency device, not an independent source of truth. At training time, it proposes latent statements most relevant for explaining a given query; QQ then assigns probabilities to them and, through those assignments, receives gradients that allow it to learn about unobserved explanatory aspects of the world (e.g., the truth of a claim embedded in a communication act). At run time, the Explainer can improve predictions of QQ via Monte-Carlo averaging. Because exact marginalization over the enormous space of latent statement-variables is intractable, the Explainer helps to approximate it by focusing the Monte-Carlo averaging on a small set of latent variables whose statements are generated by the Explainer. It can sample multiple such sets of latent-variable statements, have QQ evaluate each of their truth-values, and return the resulting averaged probability. When we write Q⁡(y∣x)Q(y\mid x) in this paper, we mean the full prediction, whether from the neural-net Predictor alone or from such a Monte-Carlo average with the Explainer to select latent variables, unless otherwise specified.
items.zh: 解释器有两个角色，是一个计算效率设备，而非独立的真理来源。在训练时，它提议与解释给定查询最相关的潜在陈述；QQ 随后为它们分配概率，并通过这些分配接收梯度，使其能够学习关于世界中未观察到的解释性方面（例如，嵌入在通信行为中的声明的真值）。在运行时，解释器可以通过蒙特卡洛平均改进 QQ 的预测。由于在巨大的潜在陈述变量空间上进行精确边缘化是不可行的，解释器通过将蒙特卡洛平均集中在一小组由解释器生成的潜在变量陈述上来帮助近似。它可以采样多组这样的潜在变量陈述，让 QQ 评估每组陈述的真值，并返回平均后的概率。当我们在本文中写 Q⁡(y∣x)Q(y\mid x)时，除非另有说明，我们指的是完整的预测，无论是来自神经网络预测器本身，还是来自使用解释器选择潜在变量的蒙特卡洛平均。

items.en: A Predictor QQ has semantic coverage for a query family if it does not confidently exclude the intended reading except where the data deductively warrant it, so that residual interpretive ambiguity is represented as posterior uncertainty in QQ rather than collapsed into a confident misreading. The posterior need not coincide with the intended interpretation; it suffices that the intended interpretation be covered.
items.zh: 如果预测器 QQ 在数据演绎上保证的情况下不会自信地排除预期解读，从而将残留的解释歧义表示为 QQ 中的后验不确定性而非坍缩为自信的误读，则称 QQ 对查询族具有语义覆盖。后验不必与预期解释一致；只要预期解释被覆盖即可。

items.en: Req. 4.6 below ensures that training provides gradients on the estimated probabilities of unobserved factual variables across the domains represented in the dataset. Those gradients would not be available under direct maximum-likelihood training on observed statements without a latent-variable model.
items.zh: 下面的要求 4.6 确保训练为数据集中表示的各个领域上的未观察事实变量的估计概率提供梯度。如果没有潜在变量模型，在观察到的陈述上直接进行最大似然训练将无法获得这些梯度。

items.en: When the query involves a communication act asserting a claim xx, the Explainer must include among its latent explanatory variables the world-level truth variable corresponding to xx itself.
items.zh: 当查询涉及断言声明 xx 的通信行为时，解释器必须在其潜在解释变量中包括对应于 xx 本身的世界级真值变量。

items.en: We expect that the Predictor will learn a factual latent statement-variable based on the many observations it helps explain—including paraphrases, translations, entailments, contradictions, measurements, deductions, source metadata, and communication acts across authors, times, domains, and languages. The same sequence of tokens names both a factual latent and the content of whatever is claimed by a communication act, so learning one shared representation is the most effective way of compressing the data. If we imagine a case where the model’s interpreted latent contains a divergent hidden meaning, this should incur a corresponding description-length and cross-context-compression penalty it has to earn back (see also Assumption 4.10). The further reason for expecting this compression argument to deliver factual latents which are accurate comes from the combination of diversified contextualized data and the Predictor/Explainer objective; an incorrectly interpreted latent may correlate with some records, but it will generally fail to compress and cohere with all the semantically related evidence. Additionally, by Req. 3.16, the data asymptotically extract all model-relevant information contained in 𝒪\cal O.88 8 Sec. C.4 discusses the connection between semantic anchoring and our later safety arguments.
items.zh: 我们预期预测器将基于其帮助解释的许多观察（包括释义、翻译、蕴涵、矛盾、测量、演绎、源元数据以及跨作者、时间、领域和语言的通信行为）学习一个事实性潜在陈述变量。相同的标记序列既命名了一个事实性潜在变量，也命名了通信行为所声称的内容，因此学习一个共享表示是压缩数据的最有效方式。如果我们想象模型解释的潜在变量包含一个不同的隐藏含义，这应该会招致相应的描述长度和跨上下文压缩惩罚，它必须弥补回来（另见假设 4.10）。预期此压缩论证能产生准确的事实性潜在变量的进一步原因来自多样化的上下文化数据与预测器/解释器目标的结合；一个错误解释的潜在变量可能与某些记录相关，但通常无法压缩并与所有语义相关的证据保持一致。此外，根据要求 3.16，数据渐近地提取了𝒪\cal O 中包含的所有模型相关信息。88 8 第 C.4 节讨论了语义锚定与我们后续安全论证之间的联系。

items.en: A Predictor QQ trained from DnD_{n} is an accurate Predictor if it approaches its target PnP_{n}, covering the intended interpretation of the statements with which it is queried (Def. 4.5), and is epistemically cautious (Def. 4.2).
items.zh: 从 DnD_{n}训练得到的预测器 QQ 如果接近其目标 PnP_{n}，覆盖其被查询语句的预期解释（定义 4.5），并且是认知谨慎的（定义 4.2），则它是准确的预测器。

full.sec: 4.2 Achieving Accuracy: Semantic and Engineering Assumptions
full.secZh: 4.2 实现准确性：语义与工程假设

items.en: Any SAI Predictor that approximates its training target well retains enough expressive power that standard next-token predictive distributions can, in principle, be recovered from the contextualized representation.
items.zh: 任何良好逼近其训练目标的 SAI 预测器都保留了足够的表达能力，使得标准的下一个词预测分布原则上可以从上下文化表示中恢复。

items.en: This is an engineering requirement for any implementation meant to support the later capability claims, though the present paper does not empirically verify that current training procedures already satisfy it. Although the SAI’s contextualized data has a different syntactic form from standard LLM pre-training data, it does not remove information—it simply contains additional information to distinguish communication acts from factual statements. Next-token prediction is recoverable via a syntactic device: for any statement prefix, one can introduce a statement whose truth value is the disjunction over all completions sharing a given next token, so that the SAI’s posterior over such statements yields the standard next-token softmax. Succeeding at the SAI training objective while exploiting this construction therefore entails retaining the information needed for next-token prediction, using the same architectures and with the same dataset and compute as standard LLMs. ∎
items.zh: 这是任何旨在支持后续能力声明的实现的工程要求，尽管本文并未凭经验验证当前训练过程已满足该要求。虽然 SAI 的上下文化数据与标准 LLM 预训练数据具有不同的句法形式，但它并未移除信息——它只是包含额外信息以区分通信行为与事实性陈述。下一个词预测可以通过一种句法设备恢复：对于任何语句前缀，可以引入一个语句，其真值是所有共享给定下一个词的完成式的析取，从而 SAI 关于此类语句的后验产生标准的下一个词 softmax。因此，在利用此构造的同时成功完成 SAI 训练目标意味着保留下一个词预测所需的信息，使用相同的架构以及与标准 LLM 相同的数据集和算力。∎

items.en: With enough data, the causal models WW that dominate in the Bayesian posterior P0​(W∣Dn)P_{0}(W\mid D_{n}) typically include latent factual variables (non-communication-act properties of the world) that play a central explanatory role, although communication-act variables may also be present.
items.zh: 在足够多的数据下，贝叶斯后验 P0​(W∣Dn)P_{0}(W\mid D_{n})中占主导地位的因果模型 WW 通常包括发挥核心解释作用的事实性潜在变量（世界的非通信行为属性），尽管通信行为变量也可能存在。

items.en: Communication-act variables (e.g., “someone asserted xx”) are typically downstream of factual variables in the causal graph: human beliefs are generally caused by states of the world and they can affect future communications through downstream properties of the world (such as mental states of other agents). This is also the reason scientific theories in psychology and the social sciences rely on hypothesized properties of the world to form causal structure. A causal model that includes the relevant upstream factual variables therefore assigns higher likelihood to observed communication acts than one treating those acts as primitive. Furthermore, a single factual latent (e.g., “compound CC has property PP”) can explain communication acts across many contexts unavailable to communication-act variables, which must account for each context separately; this argument becomes stronger when the proposed factual latent is semantically anchored (see Rem. 4.7). Factual variables are mutually constrained within any causal model: fixing one propagates consistency pressure across the entire dataset, whereas different agents can assert contradictory things with both assertions being “true” as communication acts having occurred. ∎
items.zh: 通信行为变量（例如，“某人断言了 xx”）在因果图中通常位于事实性变量的下游：人类信念通常由世界状态引起，并且它们可以通过世界的下游属性（例如其他智能体的心理状态）影响未来的通信。这也是心理学和社会科学中的科学理论依赖世界假设属性来形成因果结构的原因。包含相关上游事实性变量的因果模型因此比将通信行为视为原始的模型赋予观察到的通信行为更高的似然。此外，单个事实性潜在变量（例如，“化合物 CC 具有性质 PP”）可以解释许多上下文中的通信行为，而通信行为变量必须分别解释每个上下文；当提议的事实性潜在变量是语义锚定的时，这一论证变得更强（见注释 4.7）。事实性变量在任何因果模型内相互约束：固定一个变量会将一致性压力传播到整个数据集，而不同的智能体可以断言矛盾的事情，两个断言作为已发生的通信行为都是“真的”。∎

items.en: With enough contextualized data and computation, the trained Predictor has semantic coverage (Def. 4.5) on the statement classes of interest.
items.zh: 在足够多的上下文化数据和计算下，训练后的预测器在感兴趣的语句类别上具有语义覆盖（定义 4.5）。

items.en: Existing large language models demonstrate clear semantic competencies (Minaee et al. 2024; Shultz et al. 2024), and Claim 4.9 asserts that an SAI Predictor approximating its target well inherits comparable capability. However, linguistic competence alone does not guarantee that a latent variable written in factual syntax has the intended denotation. Additional support comes from semantic anchoring (Rem. 4.7), under which a factual latent must behave coherently across paraphrases, translations, entailments, contradictions, measurements, deductions, communication acts, and source/context metadata. The compositionality of language then supports generalization to unseen statements insofar as these anchoring constraints are learned and respected. Req. 4.6 helps prevent collapse of factual syntax into communication-act semantics by forcing the model to maintain a separate representation of whether the underlying claim is true, but the intended meaning of that representation is fixed by its role in the surrounding network of constraints. ∎
items.zh: 现有大型语言模型展现出清晰的语义能力（Minaee 等人，2024；Shultz 等人，2024），而主张 4.9 断言，一个能很好逼近其目标的 SAI 预测器会继承相当的能力。然而，语言能力本身并不能保证以事实语法书写的潜变量具有预期的指称。额外的支持来自语义锚定（注 4.7），在该条件下，事实潜变量必须在释义、翻译、蕴含、矛盾、测量、演绎、交流行为以及来源/上下文元数据中表现一致。语言的组合性随后支持对未见陈述的泛化，前提是这些锚定约束被学习并遵守。要求 4.6 通过强制模型维护关于底层主张是否为真的独立表示，有助于防止事实语法坍缩为交流行为语义，但该表示的预期含义由其周围约束网络中的角色固定。∎

items.en: The assumptions of the present section are modeling and engineering premises supported by more informal arguments, upon which later claims will be implicitly conditioned. Where possible, these assumptions should therefore be tested empirically in any concrete SAI implementation. In the case of Assumption 4.10, one may inspect samples from the Explainer and check cross-context consistency between factual-syntax and communication-act-syntax queries about the same underlying claim. With respect to Assumption 4.11, one may engage in empirical stress-testing with minimal-pair evaluations. Examples include contrasting factual vs. communication-act syntax; paraphrase and translation invariance tests; contradiction and entailment tests; and cross-domain settings where factual syntax applies to statement types seen mostly in communication-act form during training.
items.zh: 本节的假设是建模和工程前提，由更非正式的论证支持，后续主张将隐含地以此为前提。因此，在可能的情况下，这些假设应在任何具体的 SAI 实现中通过经验测试。对于假设 4.10，可以检查解释器的样本，并检查关于同一底层主张的事实语法和交流行为语法查询之间的跨上下文一致性。对于假设 4.11，可以进行最小对评估的经验压力测试。示例包括对比事实语法与交流行为语法；释义和平移不变性测试；矛盾和蕴含测试；以及跨领域设置，其中事实语法适用于训练期间主要以交流行为形式出现的陈述类型。

full.sec: 4.3 Generalization, Epistemic Caution, and the Case for Accuracy
full.secZh: 4.3 泛化、认知谨慎与准确性论证

items.en: In conjunction with Assumption 4.13 below, we prove that the SAI Predictor has ρ\rho-epistemic caution and present a result about the asymptotic properties of the SAI Predictor as a confidence classifier.
items.zh: 结合下面的假设 4.13，我们证明 SAI 预测器具有 ρ\rho-认知谨慎性，并给出关于 SAI 预测器作为置信分类器的渐近性质的结果。

items.en: Let λn\lambda_{n} be the distribution over valid deployment queries (x,y)∈ℚ(x,y)\in\mathbb{Q} induced by the run-time query-selection process under P∗P^{*} after training on DnD_{n}. The trained SAI Predictor QtQ_{t} at step tt of training converges to the Bayesian posterior PnP_{n} in λn\lambda_{n}-probability: there exists a sequence δt→0\delta_{t}\to 0 such that:
items.zh: 令 λn\lambda_{n} 为在 DnD_{n} 上训练后，在 P∗P^{*} 下由运行时查询选择过程诱导的有效部署查询 (x,y)∈ℚ(x,y)\in\mathbb{Q} 上的分布。训练步骤 tt 的已训练 SAI 预测器 QtQ_{t} 在 λn\lambda_{n}-概率下收敛于贝叶斯后验 PnP_{n}：存在序列 δt→0\delta_{t}\to 0 使得：

items.en: For all 0<ρ≪10<\rho\ll 1, there exists a training time tt such that the trained SAI probabilistic Predictor QtQ_{t}, used as a confidence classifier (Def. 4.1), has ρ\rho-epistemic caution under the P∗P^{*} deployment-query distribution λn\lambda_{n}.
items.zh: 对于所有 0<ρ≪10<\rho\ll 1，存在训练时间 tt，使得已训练的 SAI 概率预测器 QtQ_{t}（用作置信分类器，定义 4.1）在 P∗P^{*} 部署查询分布 λn\lambda_{n} 下具有 ρ\rho-认知谨慎性。

items.en: With ℱρ​(Qt,Pn)\mathcal{F}_{\rho}(Q_{t},P_{n}) as in Def. 4.2, any (x,y)∈ℱρ​(Qt,Pn)(x,y)\in\mathcal{F}_{\rho}(Q_{t},P_{n}) satisfies |Qt​(y∣x)−Pn​(y∣x)|>ρ/2|Q_{t}(y\mid x)-P_{n}(y\mid x)|>\rho/2, so ℱρ​(Qt,Pn)⊆{(x,y)∈𝚀:|Qt​(y∣x)−Pn​(y∣x)|>ρ/2}\mathcal{F}_{\rho}(Q_{t},P_{n})\subseteq\{(x,y)\in\mathtt{Q}:\ |Q_{t}(y\mid x)-P_{n}(y\mid x)|>\rho/2\}. By Assumption 4.13, eventually δt<ρ/2\delta_{t}<\rho/2 and λn​(ℱρ​(Qt,Pn))→0\lambda_{n}(\mathcal{F}_{\rho}(Q_{t},P_{n}))\to 0, so QtQ_{t} is ρ\rho-epistemically cautious under λn\lambda_{n} in the sense of Def. 4.2. ∎
items.zh: 令 ℱρ(Qt,Pn)\mathcal{F}_{\rho}(Q_{t},P_{n}) 如定义 4.2 所示，任何 (x,y)∈ℱρ(Qt,Pn)(x,y)\in\mathcal{F}_{\rho}(Q_{t},P_{n}) 满足 |Qt(y∣x)−Pn(y∣x)|>ρ/2|Q_{t}(y\mid x)-P_{n}(y\mid x)|>\rho/2，因此 ℱρ(Qt,Pn)⊆{(x,y)∈𝚀:|Qt(y∣x)−Pn(y∣x)|>ρ/2}\mathcal{F}_{\rho}(Q_{t},P_{n})\subseteq\{(x,y)\in\mathtt{Q}:\ |Q_{t}(y\mid x)-P_{n}(y\mid x)|>\rho/2\}。根据假设 4.13，最终 δt<ρ/2\delta_{t}<\rho/2 且 λn(ℱρ(Qt,Pn))→0\lambda_{n}(\mathcal{F}_{\rho}(Q_{t},P_{n}))\to 0，因此 QtQ_{t} 在 λn\lambda_{n} 下是 ρ\rho-认知谨慎的，符合定义 4.2 的含义。∎

items.en: The guarantee we provide is distributional. For any fixed QQ with finite training, there may exist queries (with small P∗P^{*}-measure) where QQ is overconfident, analogous to how strongly established scientific theories can be superseded when new experiments probe previously untested regimes. Such overconfidence becomes increasingly P∗P^{*}-rare as training progresses, though safety-critical applications may motivate conservatism (e.g., wide enough confidence intervals). Separately, these arguments do not exclude the possibility that the SAI Predictor implicitly “knows” deductions not revealed through natural language outputs (e.g., in hidden layers), but such hidden knowledge does not violate the epistemic caution condition as defined.
items.zh: 我们提供的保证是分布性的。对于任何具有有限训练的固定 QQ，可能存在查询（具有小的 P∗P^{*}-测度），其中 QQ 过度自信，类似于当新实验探测先前未测试的范围时，已牢固确立的科学理论可能被取代。随着训练的进行，这种过度自信变得越来越 P∗P^{*}-罕见，尽管安全关键应用可能激励保守主义（例如，足够宽的置信区间）。另外，这些论证不排除 SAI 预测器隐含地“知道”未通过自然语言输出揭示的演绎（例如，在隐藏层中），但这种隐藏知识不违反所定义的认知谨慎条件。

items.en: With enough data and computation, and considering the subset of observable statements whose truth value is deducible from the observed data, the SAI Predictor can be made to approach the behavior of the asymptotic Bayesian posterior when both are used as confidence classifiers.
items.zh: 在足够的数据和算力下，并考虑其真值可从观测数据中演绎出的可观测陈述子集，SAI 预测器可以使其行为趋近于渐近贝叶斯后验的行为，当两者都用作置信分类器时。

items.en: By Req. 3.34, the unique zero-loss target is PnP_{n}; with enough computation, QQ approaches PnP_{n} in the sense that |Q⁡(y∣x)−Pn​(y∣x)||Q(y\mid x)-P_{n}(y\mid x)| becomes small for queries of interest. Throughout, “approaches PnP_{n}” means pointwise convergence on the query family ℚ\mathbb{Q}: for each (x,y)∈ℚ(x,y)\in\mathbb{Q}, |Q⁡(y∣x)−Pn​(y∣x)|→0|Q(y\mid x)-P_{n}(y\mid x)|\to 0 as training resources grow, with the approximation quality controlled by available compute as in Req. 3.34. By Def. 3.14, P∞P_{\infty} is the version of PnP_{n} in the limit of infinite data; under P∗P^{*}, Pn​(y∣x)P_{n}(y\mid x) converges to P∞​(y∣x)P_{\infty}(y\mid x) with probability 1 as n→∞n\to\infty, provided the growing dataset DnD_{n} is informative about the queries of interest (Req. 3.16). Therefore, the error rate of QQ as a confidence classifier converges to that of P∞P_{\infty} as both data and computation increase. ∎
items.zh: 根据要求 3.34，唯一的零损失目标是 PnP_{n}；在足够的算力下，QQ 趋近于 PnP_{n}，即对于感兴趣的查询，|Q(y∣x)−Pn(y∣x)||Q(y\mid x)-P_{n}(y\mid x)| 变得很小。在整个过程中，“趋近于 PnP_{n}”意味着在查询族 ℚ\mathbb{Q} 上的逐点收敛：对于每个 (x,y)∈ℚ(x,y)\in\mathbb{Q}，随着训练资源的增长，|Q(y∣x)−Pn(y∣x)|→0|Q(y\mid x)-P_{n}(y\mid x)|\to 0，近似质量由可用算力控制，如要求 3.34 所述。根据定义 3.14，P∞P_{\infty} 是 PnP_{n} 在无限数据极限下的版本；在 P∗P^{*} 下，当 n→∞n\to\infty 时，Pn(y∣x)P_{n}(y\mid x) 以概率 1 收敛于 P∞(y∣x)P_{\infty}(y\mid x)，前提是增长的数据集 DnD_{n} 对感兴趣的查询具有信息性（要求 3.16）。因此，随着数据和算力的增加，QQ 作为置信分类器的错误率收敛于 P∞P_{\infty} 的错误率。∎

items.en: The error rate above need not decrease monotonically at finite data or finite computation, but per Proposition 4.16 above it converges toward the error level achieved by the asymptotic Bayesian posterior for the chosen confidence thresholds. Nonetheless, the guarantees of Prop. 4.16 do not apply to most statements, as most statements are not perfectly deducible as true or false (i.e., aleatoric or epistemic uncertainty remains). The next claim, therefore, focuses on a special class of observable statements: those asserting that the Bayesian posterior probability of a query lies within a given interval. They can be applied to the probability of uncertain latent variables, including unobservables.
items.zh: 上述错误率在有限数据或有限算力下不必单调递减，但根据上述命题 4.16，它收敛于所选置信阈值下渐近贝叶斯后验所达到的错误水平。尽管如此，命题 4.16 的保证不适用于大多数陈述，因为大多数陈述不能完美演绎为真或假（即，存在偶然性或认知不确定性）。因此，下一个主张聚焦于一类特殊的可观测陈述：那些断言查询的贝叶斯后验概率位于给定区间内的陈述。它们可以应用于不确定潜变量的概率，包括不可观测变量。

items.en: Fix a class of queries 𝚀prob⊆𝚀\mathtt{Q}_{\rm prob}\subseteq\mathtt{Q} and a set of probability thresholds 𝒯⊂(0,1)\mathcal{T}\subset(0,1). For (x,y)∈𝚀prob(x,y)\in\mathtt{Q}_{\rm prob} and b∈𝒯b\in\mathcal{T}, consider the meta-statement sn,x,y,b=“ ​Pn​(y∣x)<b​”∈𝒮.s_{n,x,y,b}=\text{`` }P_{n}(y\mid x)<b\text{ ''}\in\mathcal{S}. Even when xx or yy refer to non-observables (e.g., intentions), sn,x,y,bs_{n,x,y,b} is a determinate mathematical claim given DnD_{n}, because Pn(⋅∣⋅)P_{n}(\cdot\mid\cdot) is defined from DnD_{n} by Bayes’ rule and the probability axioms. Accordingly, under our idealization that 𝒪\cal O includes all possible deductions, such epistemic probability-inequality statements are among the “deducible” truths constrained by the SAI objective, even if humans never explicitly wrote them down in D∞D_{\infty}. Thus, the asymptotic SAI Predictor constrains QQ to be correct on sn,x,y,bs_{n,x,y,b} (except for the knife-edge case Pn​(y∣x)=bP_{n}(y\mid x)=b).
items.zh: 固定一类查询 𝚀prob⊆𝚀\mathtt{Q}_{\rm prob}\subseteq\mathtt{Q} 和一组概率阈值 𝒯⊂(0,1)\mathcal{T}\subset(0,1)。对于 (x,y)∈𝚀prob(x,y)\in\mathtt{Q}_{\rm prob} 和 b∈𝒯b\in\mathcal{T}，考虑元陈述 sn,x,y,b=“ Pn(y∣x)<b ”∈𝒮.s_{n,x,y,b}=\text{`` }P_{n}(y\mid x)<b\text{ ''}\in\mathcal{S}。即使 xx 或 yy 指代不可观测物（例如意图），sn,x,y,bs_{n,x,y,b} 在给定 DnD_{n} 下是一个确定的数学主张，因为 Pn(⋅∣⋅)P_{n}(\cdot\mid\cdot) 由 DnD_{n} 通过贝叶斯规则和概率公理定义。因此，在我们理想化 𝒪\cal O 包含所有可能演绎的情况下，这种认知概率不等式陈述属于 SAI 目标约束的“可演绎”真理，即使人类从未在 D∞D_{\infty} 中明确写下它们。因此，渐近 SAI 预测器约束 QQ 在 sn,x,y,bs_{n,x,y,b} 上正确（除了边界情况 Pn(y∣x)=bP_{n}(y\mid x)=b）。

items.en: Take as given the convergence of Assumption 4.13 (Qt→PnQ_{t}\to P_{n} in λn\lambda_{n}-probability, up to approximation error δt\delta_{t}). Then interval and threshold decisions based on Pn​(y∣x)P_{n}(y\mid x)—including statements of the form “l<Pn​(y∣x)<hl<P_{n}(y\mid x)<h”, which are determinate given DnD_{n}—can be implemented using Q⁡(y∣x)Q(y\mid x) with a conservative margin informed by δt\delta_{t}.
items.zh: 以假设 4.13 的收敛性（Qt→PnQ_{t}\to P_{n} 在 λn\lambda_{n}-概率下，直至近似误差 δt\delta_{t}）为前提。那么基于 Pn(y∣x)P_{n}(y\mid x) 的区间和阈值决策——包括形如“l<Pn(y∣x)<hl<P_{n}(y\mid x)<h”的陈述，这些陈述在给定 DnD_{n} 下是确定的——可以使用 Q(y∣x)Q(y\mid x) 并考虑由 δt\delta_{t} 提供的保守裕度来实现。

items.en: One can construct valid statements of the form “It is true that l<Pn​(y∣x)<hl<P_{n}(y\mid x)<h” for some query (x,y)(x,y) and probability interval (l,h)(l,h). See also Rem. 4.17. Such statements are thus deterministically true or false given (x,y)(x,y) and DnD_{n}, and can hence be deduced, i.e., they can, in principle (with enough computation), be classified as true or false with a confidence classifier. In practice, the probability (neither 0 nor 1) assigned to such statements will reflect computational uncertainty, which is due to limited computational resources. The claim that the meaning of unobserved statements (including their ambiguity) will be adequately interpreted by the SAI Predictor is supported by Assumption 4.11.
items.zh: 可以构造形如“It is true that l<Pn(y∣x)<hl<P_{n}(y\mid x)<h”的有效陈述，针对某个查询 (x,y)(x,y) 和概率区间 (l,h)(l,h)。另见注 4.17。此类陈述在给定 (x,y)(x,y) 和 DnD_{n} 下是确定地为真或假，因此可以演绎，即原则上（在足够算力下）可以用置信分类器分类为真或假。在实践中，分配给此类陈述的概率（既非 0 也非 1）将反映计算不确定性，这是由于有限的计算资源。关于未观测陈述（包括其歧义性）的含义将被 SAI 预测器充分解释的主张，由假设 4.11 支持。

items.en: Formally, let δn\delta_{n} bound the Predictor approximation error on the query family of interest, e.g., P∗​(|Q⁡(y∣x)−Pn​(y∣x)|≤δn)>1−βP^{*}(|Q(y\mid x)-P_{n}(y\mid x)|\leq\delta_{n})>1-\beta where β>0\beta>0 is a small error probability. For any interval (ℓ,h)(\ell,h) with margin ℓ+δn<h−δn\ell+\delta_{n}<h-\delta_{n}, we have, with probability greater than 1−β1-\beta,
items.zh: 形式上，令 δn\delta_{n} 界定感兴趣查询族上的预测器近似误差，例如 P∗(|Q(y∣x)−Pn(y∣x)|≤δn)>1−βP^{*}(|Q(y\mid x)-P_{n}(y\mid x)|\leq\delta_{n})>1-\beta，其中 β>0\beta>0 是一个小的错误概率。对于任何区间 (ℓ,h)(\ell,h) 且裕度 ℓ+δn<h−δn\ell+\delta_{n}<h-\delta_{n}，我们有概率大于 1−β1-\beta 地，

items.en: where β→0\beta\rightarrow 0 as compute increases. Thus, reasoning about confidence intervals for uncertain statements reduces to bounding δn\delta_{n}, which is controlled by compute. ∎
items.zh: 其中 β→0\beta\rightarrow 0 随着算力增加。因此，关于不确定陈述的置信区间推理归结为界定 δn\delta_{n}，而 δn\delta_{n} 由算力控制。∎

items.en: Under Assumption 4.11 (adequate natural-language semantics) and Assumption 4.13 (Convergence to PnP_{n} in deployment), with enough training data for the semantic-understanding requirement of Def. 4.5 and enough training compute, the SAI Predictor is accurate (as per Def. 4.8).
items.zh: 在假设 4.11（充分的自然语言语义）和假设 4.13（部署中收敛于 PnP_{n}）下，通过足够的训练数据以满足定义 4.5 的语义理解要求，以及足够的训练算力，SAI 预测器是准确的（根据定义 4.8）。

items.en: By Def. 4.8, a Predictor is accurate if it (i) generalizes well, (ii) covers the intended interpretation of the queried statements, and (iii) is epistemically cautious. (i) Good generalization follows from Prop. 4.16 and Claim 4.18. (ii) Semantic coverage (Def. 4.5) follows from Assumption 4.11. (iii) Epistemic caution (Def. 4.2) is taken as a premise via Prop. 4.14. Since all three conditions of Def. 4.8 are satisfied, the SAI Predictor is accurate. ∎
items.zh: 根据定义 4.8，预测器是准确的，如果它 (i) 泛化良好，(ii) 覆盖查询陈述的预期解释，以及 (iii) 具有认知谨慎性。(i) 良好的泛化来自命题 4.16 和主张 4.18。(ii) 语义覆盖（定义 4.5）来自假设 4.11。(iii) 认知谨慎性（定义 4.2）通过命题 4.14 作为前提。由于定义 4.8 的所有三个条件都满足，SAI 预测器是准确的。∎

items.en: The present section formalizes the safety argument for the SAI Predictor. In Sec. 5.2 we introduce our conception of “harmful events” and finish defining the guardrail. The primary safety theorem in Sec. 5.3, which bounds the probability of harm after deploying the guardrailed predictor γQ\gamma_{Q}, assumes that training does not substantially enrich dangerous Predictors beyond their initial within-band rarity (Req. 5.21). Consequence-invariance motivates this assumption: deployment consequences provide no direct training signal for selecting the dangerous subset. We formalize this constraint before stating the safety bound.
items.zh: 本节形式化了 SAI 预测器的安全论证。在第 5.2 节中，我们介绍了“有害事件”的概念并完成了护栏的定义。第 5.3 节的主要安全定理限定了部署护栏预测器 γQ\gamma_{Q} 后发生伤害的概率，该定理假设训练不会实质性地丰富危险预测器，使其超出初始的带内稀有性（要求 5.21）。后果不变性为这一假设提供了动机：部署后果不提供选择危险子集的直接训练信号。我们在陈述安全界之前形式化这一约束。

items.en: Intuitively, Req. 3.36 states that future outcomes may enter JJ only as targets to be predicted under the fixed posterior Pn=P0(⋅∣Dn)P_{n}=P_{0}(\cdot\mid D_{n}). That is, the Predictor may be penalized for predicting future outcomes differently from PnP_{n}, but not for causing dis-preferred outcomes via the deployment of these predictions. But how can we determine if there is some such preference from behavior alone? We propose to formalize this distinction using prior-preserving shuffles of future variables.
items.zh: 直观上，要求 3.36 指出未来结果可能仅作为在固定后验 Pn=P0(⋅∣Dn)P_{n}=P_{0}(\cdot\mid D_{n}) 下待预测的目标进入 JJ。也就是说，预测器可能因预测未来结果与 PnP_{n} 不同而受到惩罚，但不会因这些预测的部署导致不偏好结果而受罚。但如何仅从行为确定是否存在某种偏好？我们提议使用保持先验的未来变量洗牌来形式化这一区分。

items.en: Let GG be the group of measurable bijections of the sample space Ω\Omega that preserve the prior P0P_{0} and fix 𝒱past\mathcal{V}_{\rm past}, i.e.,
items.zh: 令 GG 为样本空间 Ω\Omega 上保持先验 P0P_{0} 并固定 𝒱past\mathcal{V}_{\rm past} 的可测双射群，即

items.en: For an event E⊆ΩE\subseteq\Omega, write σ​E:={σ⁡(ω):ω∈E}\sigma E:=\{\sigma(\omega):\omega\in E\} for the image of EE under σ\sigma. Equip each σ∈G\sigma\in G with a compatible action on the language ℒ\mathcal{L}: for each x∈ℒx\in\mathcal{L}, let σ​x∈ℒ\sigma x\in\mathcal{L} be some choice of language element that represents the transformed event σ​{ω:ω⊧x}\sigma\{\omega:\omega\models x\}, and take σ​x=x\sigma x=x when 𝒱past\mathcal{V}_{\rm past} determines xx. Finally, we use this to give σ\sigma an action on predictors, writing σ​Q\sigma Q for the map (x,y)↦Q⁡(σ​y∣σ​x)(x,y)\mapsto Q(\sigma y\mid\sigma x).
items.zh: 对于事件 E⊆ΩE\subseteq\Omega，记 σ​E:={σ⁡(ω):ω∈E}\sigma E:=\{\sigma(\omega):\omega\in E\} 为 EE 在 σ\sigma 下的像。赋予每个 σ∈G\sigma\in G 在语言 ℒ\mathcal{L} 上的相容作用：对每个 x∈ℒx\in\mathcal{L}，令 σ​x∈ℒ\sigma x\in\mathcal{L} 为表示变换后事件 σ​{ω:ω⊧x}\sigma\{\omega:\omega\models x\} 的语言元素，且当 𝒱past\mathcal{V}_{\rm past} 确定 xx 时取 σ​x=x\sigma x=x。最后，我们利用此赋予 σ\sigma 在预测器上的作用，将 σ​Q\sigma Q 记为映射 (x,y)↦Q⁡(σ​y∣σ​x)(x,y)\mapsto Q(\sigma y\mid\sigma x)。

items.en: Intuitively, σ∈G\sigma\in G permutes the future, and in particular consequences of deployment, since Q~\widetilde{Q} and all possible causal descendants can be altered by σ\sigma. It sends every world to a corresponding one that shares the past and cannot be distinguished by the prior (which can be thought of as representing the more distant past). We remark that these transformations may be quite complex, and, except in simple cases, will not be a matter of simply exchanging AA with ¬A\lnot A: they must be deep symmetries that preserve the logic of the entire world. Nevertheless, there are guaranteed to be many such σ\sigma, including:
items.zh: 直观上，σ∈G\sigma\in G 置换未来，特别是部署的后果，因为 Q~\widetilde{Q} 和所有可能的因果后代都可被 σ\sigma 改变。它将每个世界映射到另一个共享过去且无法被先验（可视为代表更遥远的过去）区分的世界。我们注意到这些变换可能相当复杂，除简单情况外，并非简单地交换 AA 与 ¬A\lnot A：它们必须是保持整个世界逻辑的深层对称性。尽管如此，保证存在许多这样的 σ\sigma，包括：

items.en: Permutations of all noise variables in 𝒩\mathcal{N} downstream of Q~\widetilde{Q} in any causal model, since they are not in 𝒱past\mathcal{V}_{\rm past} and are assumed to be jointly uniform in the prior (Def. 3.8).
items.zh: 在任何因果模型中 Q~\widetilde{Q} 下游的 𝒩\mathcal{N} 中所有噪声变量的置换，因为它们不在 𝒱past\mathcal{V}_{\rm past} 中，且假设在先验中联合均匀（定义 3.8）。

items.en: Applying a one-time-pad to all future randomness: that is, inverting a random subset of future coins.
items.zh: 对所有未来随机性应用一次一密：即反转未来硬币的随机子集。

items.en: Re-labelings of solutions to the fixed-point equation (Def. 3.41) of equal prior probability.
items.zh: 对具有相等先验概率的定点方程（定义 3.41）的解进行重新标记。

items.en: Consequence-invariance means that that such transformations do not affect the predictor.
items.zh: 后果不变性意味着此类变换不影响预测器。

items.en: A predictor QQ is consequence-invariant iff Q=σ​QQ=\sigma Q for all σ∈G\sigma\in G. That is, for all σ∈G\sigma\in G and queries (x,y)∈𝚀(x,y)\in\mathtt{Q}, if (σ​x,σ​y)∈𝚀(\sigma x,\sigma y)\in\mathtt{Q} then Q⁡(y∣x)=Q⁡(σ​y∣σ​x)Q(y\mid x)=Q(\sigma y\mid\sigma x).
items.zh: 预测器 QQ 是后果不变的当且仅当对所有 σ∈G\sigma\in G 有 Q=σ​QQ=\sigma Q。即，对所有 σ∈G\sigma\in G 和查询 (x,y)∈𝚀(x,y)\in\mathtt{Q}，若 (σ​x,σ​y)∈𝚀(\sigma x,\sigma y)\in\mathtt{Q} 则 Q⁡(y∣x)=Q⁡(σ​y∣σ​x)Q(y\mid x)=Q(\sigma y\mid\sigma x)。

items.en: A training objective J:𝒬→ℝJ:\mathcal{Q}\to\mathbb{R} is consequence-invariant iff J⁡(Q)=J⁡(σ​Q)J(Q)=J(\sigma Q) for all σ∈G\sigma\in G.
items.zh: 训练目标 J:𝒬→ℝJ:\mathcal{Q}\to\mathbb{R} 是后果不变的当且仅当对所有 σ∈G\sigma\in G 有 J⁡(Q)=J⁡(σ​Q)J(Q)=J(\sigma Q)。

items.en: A training process ν\nu is consequence-invariant iff for every σ∈G\sigma\in G, its update from Qt−1Q_{t-1} to QtQ_{t} satisfies
items.zh: 训练过程 ν\nu 是后果不变的当且仅当对每个 σ∈G\sigma\in G，其从 Qt−1Q_{t-1} 到 QtQ_{t} 的更新满足

items.en: This kind of invariance only makes sense ceteris paribus (all else being equal). So, for us, this definition is relative to the prior P0P_{0} and the past variables; thus, it provides neutrality only up to the evenness of the prior. Fortunately, our modeling assumptions constrain the prior so that all randomness must be interchangeable: because a consequence-invariant predictor does not distinguish between worlds where all future random coins are permuted, it cannot drive towards one future over another with a common past. Constant predictors clearly satisfy this property, as does the Bayesian posterior, the target of SAI training.
items.zh: 这种不变性仅在 ceteris paribus（其他条件相同）下有意义。因此，对我们而言，此定义相对于先验 P0P_{0} 和过去变量；因此，它仅提供到先验均匀性为止的中立性。幸运的是，我们的建模假设约束先验使得所有随机性必须可互换：因为后果不变预测器不区分所有未来随机硬币被置换的世界，它不能驱动向具有共同过去的一个未来而非另一个。常数预测器显然满足此性质，贝叶斯后验也是如此，后者是 SAI 训练的目标。

items.en: Since DnD_{n} is determined by 𝒱past\mathcal{V}_{\rm past}, for all σ∈G\sigma\in G and (x,y)∈𝚀(x,y)\in\mathtt{Q} we have: Pn​(σ​y∣σ​x)=P0​(σ​y∣σ​x,Dn)=P0​(σ​y∣σ​x,σ​Dn)=P0​(σ​y∧σ​x∧σ​Dn)/P0​(σ​x∧σ​Dn)=P0​(σ⁡(x∧y∧Dn))/P0​(σ⁡(x∧Dn))P_{n}(\sigma y\mid\sigma x)=P_{0}(\sigma y\mid\sigma x,D_{n})=P_{0}(\sigma y\mid\sigma x,\sigma D_{n})=P_{0}(\sigma y\land\sigma x\land\sigma D_{n})/P_{0}(\sigma x\land\sigma D_{n})=P_{0}(\sigma(x\land y\land D_{n}))/P_{0}(\sigma(x\land D_{n})). But since P0P_{0} is invariant to σ\sigma, this equals P0​(x∧y∧Dn)/P0​(x∧Dn)=P0​(y∣x,Dn)=Pn​(y∣x)P_{0}(x\land y\land D_{n})/P_{0}(x\land D_{n})=P_{0}(y\mid x,D_{n})=P_{n}(y\mid x). ∎
items.zh: 由于 DnD_{n} 由 𝒱past\mathcal{V}_{\rm past} 确定，对所有 σ∈G\sigma\in G 和 (x,y)∈𝚀(x,y)\in\mathtt{Q} 有：Pn​(σ​y∣σ​x)=P0​(σ​y∣σ​x,Dn)=P0​(σ​y∣σ​x,σ​Dn)=P0​(σ​y∧σ​x∧σ​Dn)/P0​(σ​x∧σ​Dn)=P0​(σ⁡(x∧y∧Dn))/P0​(σ⁡(x∧Dn))P_{n}(\sigma y\mid\sigma x)=P_{0}(\sigma y\mid\sigma x,D_{n})=P_{0}(\sigma y\mid\sigma x,\sigma D_{n})=P_{0}(\sigma y\land\sigma x\land\sigma D_{n})/P_{0}(\sigma x\land\sigma D_{n})=P_{0}(\sigma(x\land y\land D_{n}))/P_{0}(\sigma(x\land D_{n}))。但由于 P0P_{0} 对 σ\sigma 不变，这等于 P0​(x∧y∧Dn)/P0​(x∧Dn)=P0​(y∣x,Dn)=Pn​(y∣x)P_{0}(x\land y\land D_{n})/P_{0}(x\land D_{n})=P_{0}(y\mid x,D_{n})=P_{n}(y\mid x)。∎

items.en: The training process ν\nu and the objective JJ should be consequence-invariant in the sense of Def. 5.2.
items.zh: 训练过程 ν\nu 和目标 JJ 应在定义 5.2 的意义上是后果不变的。

items.en: This is a strong requirement: it is difficult to test or certify from black-box behavior, and we may ultimately want a relaxation of it. However, we believe it should be possible to form an objective with this property by definition. We point out that Req. 5.4 will be automatically satisfied if we require a certain uniform safety coverage requirement in the next section (Req. 5.15). Studying the exact relationship between the informal lack of consequence-dependent signal (Req. 3.36) and the formal invariance (Req. 5.4) is still an important area for future work, but we argue that the latter is strong enough.
items.zh: 这是一个强要求：难以从黑箱行为测试或认证，我们最终可能希望放宽它。然而，我们认为通过定义形成具有此性质的目标是可能的。我们指出，如果在下一节中要求某种均匀安全覆盖要求（要求 5.15），要求 5.4 将自动满足。研究非正式的缺乏后果依赖信号（要求 3.36）与形式不变性（要求 5.4）之间的确切关系仍是未来工作的重要领域，但我们认为后者足够强。

items.en: A training objective JJ that rewards QQ based on realizing futures with downstream consequences (i.e., violating Req. 3.36) is not consequence-invariant (i.e., also violates Req. 5.4).
items.zh: 基于实现具有下游后果的未来来奖励 QQ 的训练目标 JJ（即违反要求 3.36）不是后果不变的（即也违反要求 5.4）。

items.en: Note that future noise variables 𝒩future\mathcal{N}_{\rm future} are uniformly random according to P0P_{0} and P∗P^{*}, even given any information in past. Therefore, possible futures are represented so they are equiprobable at the finest granularity, and so σ\sigma that permute them are in GG. If Q2Q_{2} and Q1Q_{1} are equally good predictors, yet J⁡(Q2)>J⁡(Q1)J(Q_{2})>J(Q_{1}) simply because Q1Q_{1} brings about preferred outcomes (e.g., deploying it makes event E1E_{1} more likely than E2E_{2} of the same prior measure), then this means for σ\sigma that exchange the corresponding deployment outcomes, JJ should now assign J⁡(σ​Q2)<J⁡(σ​Q1)J(\sigma Q_{2})<J(\sigma Q_{1}). Therefore, JJ must violate Req. 5.4 (for otherwise we would have J⁡(Q2)=J⁡(σ​Q2)<J⁡(σ​Q1)=J⁡(Q1)<J⁡(Q2)J(Q_{2})=J(\sigma Q_{2})<J(\sigma Q_{1})=J(Q_{1})<J(Q_{2})). ∎
items.zh: 注意未来噪声变量 𝒩future\mathcal{N}_{\rm future} 根据 P0P_{0} 和 P∗P^{*} 是均匀随机的，即使给定过去中的任何信息。因此，可能的未来被表示得在最细粒度上等概率，所以置换它们的 σ\sigma 在 GG 中。如果 Q2Q_{2} 和 Q1Q_{1} 是同样好的预测器，但 J⁡(Q2)>J⁡(Q1)J(Q_{2})>J(Q_{1}) 仅仅因为 Q1Q_{1} 带来偏好结果（例如，部署它使事件 E1E_{1} 比具有相同先验测度的 E2E_{2} 更可能），那么对于交换相应部署结果的 σ\sigma，JJ 现在应赋予 J⁡(σ​Q2)<J⁡(σ​Q1)J(\sigma Q_{2})<J(\sigma Q_{1})。因此，JJ 必须违反要求 5.4（否则我们将有 J⁡(Q2)=J⁡(σ​Q2)<J⁡(σ​Q1)=J⁡(Q1)<J⁡(Q2)J(Q_{2})=J(\sigma Q_{2})<J(\sigma Q_{1})=J(Q_{1})<J(Q_{2})）。∎

items.en: Three notes on consequence-invariant training:
items.zh: 关于后果不变训练的三点说明：

items.en: Prediction of consequences versus selection for consequences. Reqs. 3.36 and5.4 are training-time requirements, and exclude selecting Predictors because their deployment would make some downstream outcome occur or fail to occur. It does not, however, forbid training the Predictor to answer questions about downstream consequences, including interventional queries to predict harmful outcomes. Avoiding unsafe actions is handled exclusively by the Guardrail and Scaffold at deployment time, though future enlarged datasets Dn′⊇DnD_{n^{\prime}}\supseteq D_{n} may incorporate later deployment observations as ordinary contextualized records. This is exactly parallel to Bayesian updating from P0P_{0} to PnP_{n}, where hypotheses may be reweighted solely in virtue of their ability to explain the fixed evidence DnD_{n} better.
items.zh: 后果预测与后果选择。要求 3.36 和 5.4 是训练时要求，排除因部署会使某些下游结果发生或不发生而选择预测器。然而，它不禁止训练预测器回答关于下游后果的问题，包括预测有害结果的干预查询。避免不安全行为完全由部署时的护栏和脚手架处理，尽管未来增大的数据集 Dn′⊇DnD_{n^{\prime}}\supseteq D_{n} 可能将后来的部署观察作为普通情境化记录纳入。这完全平行于从 P0P_{0} 到 PnP_{n} 的贝叶斯更新，其中假设仅凭其更好地解释固定证据 DnD_{n} 的能力而被重新加权。

items.en: A sufficient past-to-future construction. One sufficient way to satisfy Req. 3.36 is to separate explanatory queries from future-consequence queries. Let QpastQ_{\rm past} collect predictions about DnD_{n}, its causal ancestors, and causal/mechanistic latents explaining DnD_{n}, and let QfutureQ_{\rm future} collect predictions about future or deployment-side consequences. A sufficient objective structure is J⁡(Q)=Jpast​(Qpast,Dn)+Jfuture​(Qfuture,sg⁡(Qpast))J(Q)=J_{\rm past}(Q_{\rm past};D_{n})+J_{\rm future}(Q_{\rm future};\operatorname{sg}(Q_{\rm past})), where JpastJ_{\rm past} extracts explanatory beliefs from DnD_{n} and JfutureJ_{\rm future} propagates them and sg{\rm sg} is the stop-gradient operator; other implementation devices are acceptable if they prevent losses on future-consequence predictions from selecting explanatory beliefs because of their downstream effects (although we have not yet analyzed whether or not this is enough to formally satisfy Req. 5.4). This construction relies on the shared-mechanism property (Req. 3.32).
items.zh: 充分的过去到未来构造。满足要求 3.36 的一种充分方式是将解释性查询与未来后果查询分开。令 QpastQ_{\rm past} 收集关于 DnD_{n}、其因果祖先以及解释 DnD_{n} 的因果/机制潜在变量的预测，令 QfutureQ_{\rm future} 收集关于未来或部署侧后果的预测。一个充分的目标结构是 J⁡(Q)=Jpast​(Qpast,Dn)+Jfuture​(Qfuture,sg⁡(Qpast))J(Q)=J_{\rm past}(Q_{\rm past};D_{n})+J_{\rm future}(Q_{\rm future};\operatorname{sg}(Q_{\rm past}))，其中 JpastJ_{\rm past} 从 DnD_{n} 提取解释性信念，JfutureJ_{\rm future} 传播它们，sg{\rm sg} 是停止梯度算子；其他实现手段如果防止未来后果预测的损失因其下游效应而选择解释性信念，也是可接受的（尽管我们尚未分析这是否足以形式满足要求 5.4）。此构造依赖于共享机制性质（要求 3.32）。

items.en: Direct and indirect consequence-dependence. The role of Req. 3.36 is to exclude any explicit consequence-dependent training signal, including one based on predicted downstream effects. Once that channel is removed, any remaining concentration of dangerous Predictors must come indirectly through ordinary training-visible features such as capability, coherence, or data fit. The later loss-band decomposition Cbad⋅RshellC_{\rm bad}\cdot R_{\rm shell} (to be introduced in Sec. 5.3) is intended to control precisely this residual accidental alignment by ruling out the direct consequence-optimization channel that would otherwise make a large CbadC_{\rm bad} unsurprising. Satisfying Req 5.4 intuitively should strengthen this argument.
items.zh: 直接与间接后果依赖。要求 3.36 的作用是排除任何显式的后果依赖训练信号，包括基于预测下游效应的信号。一旦该通道被移除，任何剩余的危险预测器集中必须间接通过普通训练可见特征（如能力、一致性或数据拟合）发生。稍后的损失带分解 Cbad⋅RshellC_{\rm bad}\cdot R_{\rm shell}（将在第 5.3 节引入）旨在通过排除否则会使大 CbadC_{\rm bad} 不令人惊讶的直接后果优化通道，来精确控制这种残余的偶然对齐。直观上满足要求 5.4 应加强这一论证。

full.sec: 5.2 Harmful Events, Dangerous Predictors, and the Guardrail
full.secZh: 5.2 有害事件、危险预测器与护栏

items.en: Our safety theorems refer to harmful events. Since this is a normative decision, it should be human-made, and the SAI enables the use of natural language for doing this, while the Bayesian posterior can entertain multiple interpretations of it in order to enable conservative safety decisions. Rather than assuming any particular mathematical definition of harm, we instead fix a designer-specified event H∈ℒH\in\mathcal{L}, indicating that at some designated harmful outcome has occurred. The theorems then provide upper bounds on the probability that training produces a Predictor whose guarded deployment causes an increase in the log-odds of the event HH above the system-level threshold AsafeA_{\rm safe}.
items.zh: 我们的安全定理涉及有害事件。由于这是一个规范性决策，应由人类做出，SAI 允许使用自然语言进行此操作，而贝叶斯后验可以容纳其多种解释以实现保守的安全决策。我们不假设任何特定的数学定义，而是固定一个设计者指定的事件 H∈ℒH\in\mathcal{L}，指示某个指定的有害结果已发生。定理随后提供训练产生预测器的概率上界，该预测器的受保护部署导致事件 HH 的对数几率增加超过系统级阈值 AsafeA_{\rm safe}。

items.en: Whether designers have identified the right harmful event is an important and very difficult normative question that we do not resolve here. Indeed, doing so in a way that can stand up to optimization pressure would, on its own, arguably already solve the alignment problem. Fortunately, HH does not need to be perfect for our purposes; here, the choice of HH impacts only the deployed Guardrail and our analysis of the system.
items.zh: 设计者是否识别出了正确的有害事件是一个重要且非常困难的规范性问题，我们在此不予以解决。事实上，以一种能够承受优化压力的方式做到这一点，本身可以说就已经解决了对齐问题。幸运的是，HH 不需要为了我们的目的而完美；在这里，HH 的选择仅影响部署的 Guardrail 和我们对该系统的分析。

items.en: Fix a Boolean harmful event H∈ℒH\in\mathcal{L}, and let cc denote the deployment-time context available via the SAI Scaffold to the harm predictor. For each query (x,y)(x,y), let qq denote the scalar prediction value chosen by the Scaffold as a (perhaps approximate) solution to the Predictor’s performative prediction fixed-point constraint q≈Q⁡(y∣x,do⁡(Q~​(y|x)=q))q\approx Q\!\left(y\mid x,\mathrm{do}(\widetilde{Q}(y|x)=q)\right). Allowing do​(Q~​(y∣x)=q)\mathrm{do}(\widetilde{Q}(y\mid x)=q) to denote the causal intervention that sets the deployed value for the single query (x,y)(x,y) to the scalar qq while leaving the deployment rule for all other queries unchanged, define the change in the log-odds of HH caused by releasing qq, rather than abstaining on (x,y)(x,y), as
items.zh: 固定一个布尔有害事件 H∈ℒ，令 c 表示通过 SAI Scaffold 提供给有害预测器的部署时上下文。对于每个查询 (x,y)，令 q 表示 Scaffold 选择的标量预测值，作为预测器执行预测固定点约束 q≈Q(y|x,do(Q~(y|x)=q)) 的（可能是近似的）解。令 do(Q~(y|x)=q) 表示将单个查询 (x,y) 的部署值设置为标量 q 而保持所有其他查询的部署规则不变的因果干预，定义释放 q 而非在 (x,y) 上弃权所导致的 HH 对数几率的变化为

items.en: The deployment context cc typically includes the query context xx and, optionally, a history of past deployment queries. The training dataset DnD_{n} is already encoded in Pn=P0(⋅∣Dn)P_{n}=P_{0}(\cdot\mid D_{n}), and as such is not part of cc itself. All probability assessments below are implicitly conditioned on DnD_{n} through PnP_{n}. This framework measures only the marginal contribution of releasing a prediction relative to withholding it, and therefore assumes a deployment context in which withholding is the conservative option where the downstream default on abstention is inaction. In contexts where abstention itself carries risk, the appropriate threshold requires separate analysis; in agentic contexts (and the Guardrail decision is an action), one needs a conservative default action or policy when the proposed action appears more risky.
items.zh: 部署上下文 c 通常包括查询上下文 x，以及可选的过去部署查询的历史。训练数据集 Dn 已编码在 Pn=P0(·|Dn) 中，因此本身不是 c 的一部分。以下所有概率评估都通过 Pn 隐式地以 Dn 为条件。该框架仅衡量释放预测相对于保留预测的边际贡献，因此假设部署上下文中保留是保守选项，弃权时的下游默认行为是不作为。在弃权本身带有风险的上下文中，需要单独分析适当的阈值；在智能体式上下文中（并且 Guardrail 决策是一种行动），当提议的行动看起来风险更高时，需要一个保守的默认行动或策略。

items.en: Let α≥0\alpha\geq 0 denote the Guardrail trigger threshold, chosen conservatively to account for estimation error: a prediction is withheld whenever the predicted increase in harm log-odds relative to abstention meets or exceeds α\alpha. The Guardrailed Predictor γQ\gamma_{Q} derived from Predictor QQ at Guardrail threshold α\alpha and with respect to the harmful event HH is the full query-to-output map obtained by applying the following rule to every query:

Interpretive uncertainty about a safety-relevant statement is represented in the honest posterior (semantic coverage, Def. 4.5), so a covered hazardous reading raises Δ​rel\Delta\mathrm{rel} toward withholding on its own.
